revision.comment: [[WP:AES|←]]新しいページ: '{{日本の古墳| 名称=墨小盛田古墳| 所在地=千葉県印旛郡酒々町墨字盛田36-1| 形状=方墳| 規模=30m×23m| 築造年代=7世紀末～...'
revision.text:
{{日本の古墳| 名称=墨小盛田古墳| 所在地=千葉県印旛郡酒々町墨字盛田36-1| 形状=方墳| 規模=30m×23m| 築造年代=7世紀末～8世紀|}} '''墨小盛田古墳'''（すみこもったこふん）は、[[千葉県]][[印旛郡]][[酒々町]]にある[[方墳]]である。
==概要== 南北辺約30m、東西辺約23m、高さ3メートルの長方墳である。
総合公園の建設に伴い昭和61年11月から約1ヶ月間の調査が行われた。幅2メールの周溝が巡り、張り出しが付く。主体部は墳丘南中央で箱式石棺が確認された。テラス部から出土した須恵器より7世紀末から8世紀の築造と考えられている。昭和62年2月に復元工事が行われた。
== 参考文献 == 千葉県教育委員会 「千葉県記念物実態調査報告書Ⅲ」 1995年
== 関連項目 == *[[日本の古墳一覧]]
{{japanese-history-stub}} {{デフォルトソート:すみこもつたこふん}} [[Category:千葉県の古墳]] [[Category:酒々町]] [[Category:方墳]]

{{日本の古墳| 名称=墨小盛田古墳| 所在地=千葉県印旛郡酒々町墨字盛田36-1| 形状=方墳| 規模=30m×23m| 築造年代=7世紀末～8世紀|}} '''墨小盛田古墳'''（すみこもったこふん）は、[[千葉県]][[印旛郡]][[酒々町]]にある[[方墳]]である。
==概要== 南北辺約30m、東西辺約23m、高さ3メートルの長方墳である。
総合公園の建設に伴い昭和61年11月から約1ヶ月間の調査が行われた。幅2メールの周溝が巡り、張り出しが付く。主体部は墳丘南中央で箱式石棺が確認された。テラス部から出土した須恵器より7世紀末から8世紀の築造と考えられている。昭和62年2月に復元工事が行われた。
== 参考文献 == 酒々町・印旛郡市文化財センター 「酒々町総合公園遺跡発掘調査報告書 -尾上広畑遺跡・墨小盛田古墳- 」 1987年

revision.comment: 地図を加えてみました。
revision.text:
{{日本の古墳 |名称=墨小盛田古墳 |画像 =[[File:Sumikomorida Kofun.JPG|250px]] |画像キャプション =墳丘 |所在地=千葉県印旛郡酒々井町墨字盛田36-1 |史跡=指定なし |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|43|18.3|N|140|16|46.0|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} |形状=方墳 |規模=30メートル×23メートル |築造年代=7世紀末-8世紀 |地図2 = {{Location map|Japan Chiba|label=墨小盛田
古墳|lat_deg=35|lat_min=43|lat_sec=18.3|lon_deg=140|lon_min=16|lon_sec=46.0|mark=方墳.png|marksize=25|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=千葉県内の位置}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord2|35|43|18.3|N|140|16|46.0|E}}}} }} '''墨小盛田古墳'''（すみこもりだこふん/すみこもったこふん{{Cite web|author=酒々井町役場|url=https://www.town.shisui.chiba.jp/navi/mobile/id/374.html|title=墨小盛田古墳|work=酒々井観光ナビ|accessdate=2020-08-20}}）は、[[千葉県]][[印旛郡]][[酒々井町]]墨にある[[方墳]]である。 ==概要== 南北辺約30メートル、東西辺約23メートル、高さ3メートルの長方墳である。長方墳は、[[古墳時代]]の末に[[下総]]地域にみられる特徴的な古墳である。 総合公園の建設に伴い1986年（昭和61年）11月から約1か月間の調査が行われた。幅2メールの周溝が巡り、張り出しが付く。主体部は墳丘南中央で[[横口式石槨]]が確認された。テラス部から出土した[[須恵器]]より[[7世紀]]末から[[8世紀]]の築造と考えられている。1987年（昭和62年）2月に復元工事が行われた。 == 脚注 == {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == * 酒々井町・印旛郡市文化財センター 「酒々井町総合公園遺跡発掘調査報告書 -尾上広畑遺跡・墨小盛田古墳- 」 1987年 == 関連項目 == *[[日本の古墳一覧]] {{デフォルトソート:すみこもりたこふん}} [[Category:千葉県の古墳]] [[Category:酒々井町]] [[Category:長方形墳]] {{japanese-history-stub}}

revision.text:
{{日本の古墳 |名称=墨小盛田古墳 |画像 =[[File:Sumikomorida Kofun.JPG|250px]] |画像キャプション =墳丘 |所在地=千葉県印旛郡酒々井町墨字盛田36-1 |史跡=指定なし |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|43|18.3|N|140|16|46.0|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} |形状=方墳 |規模=30メートル×23メートル |築造年代=7世紀末-8世紀 |地図2 = {{Location map|Japan Chiba|label=墨小盛田
古墳|lat_deg=35|lat_min=43|lat_sec=18.3|lon_deg=140|lon_min=16|lon_sec=46.0|mark=方墳.png|marksize=25|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=千葉県内の位置}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord2|35|43|18.3|N|140|16|46.0|E}}}} }} '''墨小盛田古墳'''（すみこもりだこふん/すみこもったこふん{{Cite web|author=酒々井町役場|url=https://www.town.shisui.chiba.jp/navi/mobile/id/374.html|title=墨小盛田古墳|work=酒々井観光ナビ|accessdate=2020-08-20}}）は、[[千葉県]][[印旛郡]][[酒々井町]]墨にある[[方墳]]である。 ==概要== 南北辺約30メートル、東西辺約23メートル、高さ3メートルの長方墳である。長方墳は、[[古墳時代]]の末に[[下総]]地域にみられる特徴的な古墳である。 [[酒々井総合公園]]の建設に伴い1986年（昭和61年）11月から約1か月間の調査が行われた。幅2メールの周溝が巡り、張り出しが付く。主体部は墳丘南中央で[[横口式石槨]]が確認された。テラス部から出土した[[須恵器]]より[[7世紀]]末から[[8世紀]]の築造と考えられている。1987年（昭和62年）2月に復元工事が行われた。 == 脚注 == {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == * 酒々井町・印旛郡市文化財センター 「酒々井町総合公園遺跡発掘調査報告書 -尾上広畑遺跡・墨小盛田古墳- 」 1987年 == 関連項目 == *[[日本の古墳一覧]] {{デフォルトソート:すみこもりたこふん}} [[Category:千葉県の古墳]] [[Category:酒々井町]] [[Category:長方形墳]] {{japanese-history-stub}}

revision.comment: Created page with "[[ja:墨小盛田古墳]]"
revision.text: [[ja:墨小盛田古墳]]

[[ja:墨小盛田古墳]] {{日本の古墳 |名称 = Sumikomorida Kofun |画像 = [[File:Sumikomorida Kofun.JPG|250px]] |画像キャプション = Burial mound |所在地 = {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}}, {{ill|Inba District, Chiba|ja|印旛郡}}, {{ill|Shisui, Chiba|ja|酒々井町}}, {{ill|Sumi (Shisui)|ja|墨}} Aza Morita 36-1 |史跡 = None |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|43|18.3|N|140|16|46.0|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} |形状 = Rectangular burial mound |規模 = 30m (north-south) × 23m (east-west) |築造年代 = Late 7th century to 8th century |地図2 = {{Location map|Japan Chiba|label=Sumikomorida
Kofun|lat_deg=35|lat_min=43|lat_sec=18.3|lon_deg=140|lon_min=16|lon_sec=46.0|mark=方墳.svg|marksize=25|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=Location within Chiba Prefecture}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|43|18.3|N|140|16|46.0|E}}}} }} '''Sumikomorida Kofun''' (墨小盛田古墳, also pronounced as すみこもったこふん{{Cite web|和書|author=Shisui Town Hall|url=https://www.town.shisui.chiba.jp/navi/mobile/id/374.html|title=Sumikomorida Kofun|work=Shisui Tourism Navi|accessdate=2020-08-20}}) is a {{ill|rectangular burial mound|ja|方墳}} located in {{ill|Shisui, Chiba|ja|酒々井町}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}}, Japan. == Overview == The burial mound measures approximately 30 meters north-south, 23 meters east-west, and 3 meters in height. Rectangular burial mounds are a characteristic type of kofun found in the {{ill|Shimousa Province|ja|下総国}} region during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. In November 1986, during the construction of the {{ill|Shisui Comprehensive Park|ja|酒々井総合公園}}, a one-month excavation was conducted. The mound features a 2-meter-wide surrounding moat and a protruding section. The main burial chamber, located at the center of the southern side of the mound, was identified as a {{ill|horizontal stone burial chamber|ja|横口式石槨}}. Based on the {{ill|sue ware|ja|須恵器}} found on the terrace section, the kofun is believed to have been constructed in the late 7th to early 8th century. Restoration work was carried out in February 1987. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * Shisui Town and Inba District Cultural Property Center. _Excavation Report on the Shisui Town Comprehensive Park Site: Onoue Hirohata Ruins and Sumikomorida Kofun._ 1987. == Related Topics == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}} {{japanese-history-stub}} [[Category:Kofun in Chiba Prefecture]] [[Category:Rectangular burial mounds]] [[Category:History of Shisui, Chiba]]

The main burial chamber, located at the center of the southern side of the mound, was identified as a {{ill|horizontal stone burial chamber|ja|横口式石槨}}. Based on the {{ill|sue ware|ja|須恵器}} found on the terrace section, the kofun is believed to have been constructed in the late 7th to early 8th century. Restoration work was carried out in February 1987. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * Shisui Town and Inba District Cultural Property Center. _Excavation Report on the Shisui Town Comprehensive Park Site: Onoue Hirohata Ruins and Sumikomorida Kofun._ 1987. == Related Topics == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}} {{japanese-history-stub}} [[Category:Kofun in Chiba Prefecture]] [[Category:Rectangular burial mounds]] [[Category:History of Shisui, Chiba]]

Rectangular burial mounds are a characteristic type of kofun found in the {{ill|Shimousa Province|ja|下総国}} region during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. In November 1986, during the construction of the {{ill|Shisui Comprehensive Park|ja|酒々井総合公園}}, a one-month excavation was conducted. The mound features a 2-meter-wide surrounding moat and a protruding section. The main burial chamber, located at the center of the southern side of the mound, was identified as a {{ill|horizontal stone burial chamber|ja|横口式石槨}}. Based on the {{ill|sue ware|ja|須恵器}} found on the terrace section, the kofun is believed to have been constructed in the late 7th to early 8th century. Restoration work was carried out in February 1987. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * Shisui Town and Inba District Cultural Property Center. _Excavation Report on the Shisui Town Comprehensive Park Site: Onoue Hirohata Ruins and Sumikomorida Kofun._ 1987. == Related Topics == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧}} {{japanese-history-stub}} [[Category:Kofun in Chiba Prefecture]] [[Category:Rectangular burial mounds]] [[Category:History of Shisui, Chiba]] [[Category:pages to try to fix ills]]

Kofun|lat_deg=35|lat_min=43|lat_sec=18.3|lon_deg=140|lon_min=16|lon_sec=46.0|mark=方墳.svg|marksize=25|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location within Chiba Prefecture}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|43|18.3|N|140|16|46.0|E}}}} }} '''Sumikomorida Kofun''' (墨小盛田古墳, also pronounced as すみこもったこふん{{Cite web|和書|author=Shisui Town Hall|url=https://www.town.shisui.chiba.jp/navi/mobile/id/374.html|title=Sumikomorida Kofun|work=Shisui Tourism Navi|accessdate=2020-08-20}}) is a {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳}} located in {{ill|Shisui, Chiba|ja|酒々井町}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}}, Japan. == Overview == The burial mound measures approximately 30 meters north-south, 23 meters east-west, and 3 meters in height. Rectangular burial mounds are a characteristic type of kofun found in the {{ill|Shimousa Province|ja|下総国}} region during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. In November 1986, during the construction of the {{ill|Shisui Comprehensive Park|ja|酒々井総合公園}}, a one-month excavation was conducted. The mound features a 2-meter-wide surrounding moat and a protruding section. The main burial chamber, located at the center of the southern side of the mound, was identified as a {{ill|horizontal stone burial chamber|ja|横口式石槨}}. Based on the {{ill|sue ware|ja|須恵器}} found on the terrace section, the kofun is believed to have been constructed in the late 7th to early 8th century. Restoration work was carried out in February 1987. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * Shisui Town and Inba District Cultural Property Center. _Excavation Report on the Shisui Town Comprehensive Park Site: Onoue Hirohata Ruins and Sumikomorida Kofun._ 1987. == Related Topics == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧}} {{japanese-history-stub}} [[Category:Kofun in Chiba Prefecture]] [[Category:Rectangular burial mounds]] [[Category:History of Shisui, Chiba]] [[Category:kk]][[category:kk]] [[Category:attempting to force efresh]]

Kofun|lat_deg=35|lat_min=43|lat_sec=18.3|lon_deg=140|lon_min=16|lon_sec=46.0|mark=方墳.svg|marksize=25|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location within Chiba Prefecture}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|43|18.3|N|140|16|46.0|E}}}} }} '''Sumikomorida Kofun''' (墨小盛田古墳, also pronounced as すみこもったこふん{{Cite web|和書|author=Shisui Town Hall|url=https://www.town.shisui.chiba.jp/navi/mobile/id/374.html|title=Sumikomorida Kofun|work=Shisui Tourism Navi|accessdate=2020-08-20}}) is a {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳}} located in {{ill|Shisui, Chiba|ja|酒々井町}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}}, Japan. == Overview == The burial mound measures approximately 30 meters north-south, 23 meters east-west, and 3 meters in height. Rectangular burial mounds are a characteristic type of kofun found in the {{ill|Shimousa Province|ja|下総国}} region during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. In November 1986, during the construction of the {{ill|Shisui Comprehensive Park|ja|酒々井総合公園}}, a one-month excavation was conducted. The mound features a 2-meter-wide surrounding moat and a protruding section. The main burial chamber, located at the center of the southern side of the mound, was identified as a {{ill|horizontal stone burial chamber|ja|横口式石槨}}. Based on the {{ill|sue ware|ja|須恵器}} found on the terrace section, the kofun is believed to have been constructed in the late 7th to early 8th century. Restoration work was carried out in February 1987. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * Shisui Town and Inba District Cultural Property Center. _Excavation Report on the Shisui Town Comprehensive Park Site: Onoue Hirohata Ruins and Sumikomorida Kofun._ 1987. == Related Topics == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧}} {{japanese-history-stub}} [[Category:Kofun in Chiba Prefecture]] [[Category:Rectangular burial mounds]] [[Category:History of Shisui, Chiba]] [[Category:kk]][[category:kk]] [[Category:attempting to force efresh]] [[Category:bump 1-20]] [[Category:bump 1-20 (2)]]

[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:pages needing attribution]][[Category:Sumiko Morita Kofun]][[Category:pages needing attribution]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Sumiko Morita Kofun}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:pages needing attribution]][[Category:Sumiko Morita Kofun]][[Category:pages needing attribution]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Sumiko Morita Kofun}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:pages needing attribution]][[Category:Sumiko Morita Kofun]][[Category:pages needing attribution]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Sumiko Morita Kofun}}[[ja:墨小盛田古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site |name = Sumikomorida Kofun |image = [[File:Sumikomorida Kofun.JPG|250px]] |imageキャプション = Burial mound |所在地 = {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}}, {{ill|Inba District, Chiba|ja|印旛郡}}, {{ill|Shisui, Chiba|ja|酒々井町}}, {{ill|Sumi (Shisui)|ja|墨}} Aza Morita 36-1 |史跡 = None |coordinates = {{coordinates|35|43|18.3|N|140|16|46.0|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} |形状 = Rectangular burial mound |規模 = 30m (north-south) × 23m (east-west) |築造年代 = Late 7th century to 8th century |地図2 = {{location map|Japan Chiba|label=Sumikomorida
Kofun|lat_deg=35|lat_min=43|lat_sec=18.3|lon_deg=140|lon_min=16|lon_sec=46.0|mark=方墳.svg|marksize=25|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location within Chiba Prefecture}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|43|18.3|N|140|16|46.0|E}}}} }} '''Sumikomorida Kofun''' (墨小盛田古墳, also pronounced as すみこもったこふん{{Cite web|和書|author=Shisui Town Hall|url=https://www.town.shisui.chiba.jp/navi/mobile/id/374.html|title=Sumikomorida Kofun|work=Shisui Tourism Navi|accessdate=2020-08-20}}) is a {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳}} located in {{ill|Shisui, Chiba|ja|酒々井町}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}}, Japan. == Overview == The burial mound measures approximately 30 meters north-south, 23 meters east-west, and 3 meters in height. Rectangular burial mounds are a characteristic type of kofun found in the {{ill|Shimousa Province|ja|下総国}} region during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. In November 1986, during the construction of the {{ill|Shisui Comprehensive Park|ja|酒々井総合公園}}, a one-month excavation was conducted. The mound features a 2-meter-wide surrounding moat and a protruding section. The main burial chamber, located at the center of the southern side of the mound, was identified as a {{ill|horizontal stone burial chamber|ja|横口式石槨}}. Based on the {{ill|sue ware|ja|須恵器}} found on the terrace section, the kofun is believed to have been constructed in the late 7th to early 8th century. Restoration work was carried out in February 1987. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * Shisui Town and Inba District Cultural Property Center. _Excavation Report on the Shisui Town Comprehensive Park Site: Onoue Hirohata Ruins and Sumikomorida Kofun._ 1987. == Related Topics == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧}} {{japanese-history-stub}} [[Category:Kofun in Chiba Prefecture]] [[Category:Rectangular Kofun]] [[Category:History of Shisui, Chiba]] [[Category:attempting to force efresh]] [[Category:bump 1-20 (2)]]

Kofun|lat_deg=35|lat_min=43|lat_sec=18.3|lon_deg=140|lon_min=16|lon_sec=46.0|mark=方墳.svg|marksize=25|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location within Chiba Prefecture}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|43|18.3|N|140|16|46.0|E}}}} }} '''Sumikomorida Kofun''' (墨小盛田古墳, also pronounced as すみこもったこふん{{Cite web|和書|author=Shisui Town Hall|url=https://www.town.shisui.chiba.jp/navi/mobile/id/374.html|title=Sumikomorida Kofun|work=Shisui Tourism Navi|accessdate=2020-08-20}}) is a {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳}} located in {{ill|Shisui, Chiba|ja|酒々井町}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}}, Japan. == Overview == The burial mound measures approximately 30 meters north-south, 23 meters east-west, and 3 meters in height. Rectangular burial mounds are a characteristic type of kofun found in the {{ill|Shimousa Province|ja|下総国}} region during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. In November 1986, during the construction of the {{ill|Shisui Comprehensive Park|ja|酒々井総合公園}}, a one-month excavation was conducted. The mound features a 2-meter-wide surrounding moat and a protruding section. The main burial chamber, located at the center of the southern side of the mound, was identified as a {{ill|horizontal stone burial chamber|ja|横口式石槨}}. Based on the {{ill|sue ware|ja|須恵器}} found on the terrace section, the kofun is believed to have been constructed in the late 7th to early 8th century. Restoration work was carried out in February 1987. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * Shisui Town and Inba District Cultural Property Center. _Excavation Report on the Shisui Town Comprehensive Park Site: Onoue Hirohata Ruins and Sumikomorida Kofun._ 1987. == Related Topics == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧}} {{japanese-history-stub}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kofun in Chiba Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Rectangular Kofun]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:History of Shisui, Chiba]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:attempting to force efresh]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:bump 1-20 (2)]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Rectangular Kofun]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Sumiko Morita Kofun]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Rectangular Kofun]]

Chiba|ja|印旛郡}}, {{ill|Shisui, Chiba|ja|酒々井町}}, {{ill|Sumi (Shisui)|ja|墨}} Aza Morita 36-1 |史跡 = None |coordinates = {{coordinates|35|43|18.3|N|140|16|46.0|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} |形状 = Rectangular burial mound |規模 = 30m (north-south) × 23m (east-west) |築造年代 = Late 7th century to 8th century |地図2 = {{location map|Japan Chiba|label=Sumikomorida
Kofun|lat_deg=35|lat_min=43|lat_sec=18.3|lon_deg=140|lon_min=16|lon_sec=46.0|mark=方墳.svg|marksize=25|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location within Chiba Prefecture}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|43|18.3|N|140|16|46.0|E}}}} }} '''Sumikomorida Kofun''' (墨小盛田古墳, also pronounced as すみこもったこふん{{Cite web|和書|author=Shisui Town Hall|url=https://www.town.shisui.chiba.jp/navi/mobile/id/374.html|title=Sumikomorida Kofun|work=Shisui Tourism Navi|accessdate=2020-08-20}}) is a {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳}} located in {{ill|Shisui, Chiba|ja|酒々井町}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}}, Japan. == Overview == The burial mound measures approximately 30 meters north-south, 23 meters east-west, and 3 meters in height. Rectangular burial mounds are a characteristic type of kofun found in the {{ill|Shimousa Province|ja|下総国}} region during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. In November 1986, during the construction of the {{ill|Shisui Comprehensive Park|ja|酒々井総合公園}}, a one-month excavation was conducted. The mound features a 2-meter-wide surrounding moat and a protruding section. The main burial chamber, located at the center of the southern side of the mound, was identified as a {{ill|horizontal stone burial chamber|ja|横口式石槨}}. Based on the {{ill|sue ware|ja|須恵器}} found on the terrace section, the kofun is believed to have been constructed in the late 7th to early 8th century. Restoration work was carried out in February 1987. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * Shisui Town and Inba District Cultural Property Center. _Excavation Report on the Shisui Town Comprehensive Park Site: Onoue Hirohata Ruins and Sumikomorida Kofun._ 1987. == Related Topics == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧}} {{japanese-history-stub}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kofun in Chiba Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Rectangular Kofun]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:History of Shisui, Chiba]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:attempting to force efresh]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:bump 1-20 (2)]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Rectangular Kofun]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Sumiko Morita Kofun]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Rectangular Kofun]] [[Category:kk]]{{Kofun navbar}}[[Category:kk]]

Rectangular burial mounds are a characteristic type of kofun found in the {{ill|Shimousa Province|ja|下総国}} region during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. In November 1986, during the construction of the {{ill|Shisui Comprehensive Park|ja|酒々井総合公園}}, a one-month excavation was conducted. The mound features a 2-meter-wide surrounding moat and a protruding section. The main burial chamber, located at the center of the southern side of the mound, was identified as a {{ill|horizontal stone burial chamber|ja|横口式石槨}}. Based on the {{ill|sue ware|ja|須恵器}} found on the terrace section, the kofun is believed to have been constructed in the late 7th to early 8th century. Restoration work was carried out in February 1987. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * Shisui Town and Inba District Cultural Property Center. _Excavation Report on the Shisui Town Comprehensive Park Site: Onoue Hirohata Ruins and Sumikomorida Kofun._ 1987. == Related Topics == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧}} {{japanese-history-stub}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kofun in Chiba Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Rectangular Kofun]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:History of Shisui, Chiba]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:attempting to force efresh]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:bump 1-20 (2)]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Rectangular Kofun]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Sumiko Morita Kofun]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Rectangular Kofun]] [[Category:kk]] {{Kofun navbar}} [[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Sumiko Morita Kofun]][[Category:kk]]

Sumikomorida Kofun |image = [[File:Sumikomorida Kofun.JPG|250px]] |imageキャプション = Burial mound |所在地 = {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}}, {{ill|Inba District, Chiba|ja|印旛郡}}, {{ill|Shisui, Chiba|ja|酒々井町}}, {{ill|Sumi (Shisui)|ja|墨}} Aza Morita 36-1 |史跡 = None |coordinates = {{coordinates|35|43|18.3|N|140|16|46.0|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} |形状 = Rectangular burial mound |規模 = 30m (north-south) × 23m (east-west) |築造年代 = Late 7th century to 8th century |地図2 = {{location map|Japan Chiba|label=Sumikomorida
Kofun|lat_deg=35|lat_min=43|lat_sec=18.3|lon_deg=140|lon_min=16|lon_sec=46.0|mark=方墳.svg|marksize=25|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location within Chiba Prefecture}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|43|18.3|N|140|16|46.0|E}}}} }} '''Sumikomorida Kofun''' (墨小盛田古墳, also pronounced as すみこもったこふん{{Cite web|和書|author=Shisui Town Hall|url=https://www.town.shisui.chiba.jp/navi/mobile/id/374.html|title=Sumikomorida Kofun|work=Shisui Tourism Navi|accessdate=2020-08-20}}) is a {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳}} located in {{ill|Shisui, Chiba|ja|酒々井町}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}}, Japan. == Overview == The burial mound measures approximately 30 meters north-south, 23 meters east-west, and 3 meters in height. Rectangular burial mounds are a characteristic type of kofun found in the {{ill|Shimousa Province|ja|下総国}} region during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. In November 1986, during the construction of the {{ill|Shisui Comprehensive Park|ja|酒々井総合公園}}, a one-month excavation was conducted. The mound features a 2-meter-wide surrounding moat and a protruding section. The main burial chamber, located at the center of the southern side of the mound, was identified as a {{ill|horizontal stone burial chamber|ja|横口式石槨}}. Based on the {{ill|sue ware|ja|須恵器}} found on the terrace section, the kofun is believed to have been constructed in the late 7th to early 8th century. Restoration work was carried out in February 1987. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * Shisui Town and Inba District Cultural Property Center. _Excavation Report on the Shisui Town Comprehensive Park Site: Onoue Hirohata Ruins and Sumikomorida Kofun._ 1987. == Related Topics == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧}} {{japanese-history-stub}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kofun in Chiba Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Rectangular Kofun]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:History of Shisui, Chiba]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:attempting to force efresh]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:bump 1-20 (2)]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Rectangular Kofun]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Sumiko Morita Kofun]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Rectangular Kofun]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] {{Kofun navbar}} [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Sumiko Morita Kofun]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:Sumiko Morita Kofun]]

Restoration work was carried out in February 1987. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * Shisui Town and Inba District Cultural Property Center. _Excavation Report on the Shisui Town Comprehensive Park Site: Onoue Hirohata Ruins and Sumikomorida Kofun._ 1987. == Related Topics == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧}} {{japanese-history-stub}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kofun in Chiba Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Rectangular Kofun]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:History of Shisui, Chiba]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:attempting to force efresh]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:bump 1-20 (2)]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Rectangular Kofun]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Sumiko Morita Kofun]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Rectangular Kofun]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] {{Kofun navbar}} [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Sumiko Morita Kofun]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:Sumiko Morita Kofun]] [[Category:new pages]]

The main burial chamber, located at the center of the southern side of the mound, was identified as a {{ill|horizontal stone burial chamber|ja|横口式石槨}}. Based on the {{ill|sue ware|ja|須恵器}} found on the terrace section, the kofun is believed to have been constructed in the late 7th to early 8th century. Restoration work was carried out in February 1987. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * Shisui Town and Inba District Cultural Property Center. _Excavation Report on the Shisui Town Comprehensive Park Site: Onoue Hirohata Ruins and Sumikomorida Kofun._ 1987. == Related Topics == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧}} {{japanese-history-stub}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kofun in Chiba Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Rectangular Kofun]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:History of Shisui, Chiba]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:attempting to force efresh]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:bump 1-20 (2)]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Rectangular Kofun]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Sumiko Morita Kofun]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Rectangular Kofun]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] {{Kofun navbar}} [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Sumiko Morita Kofun]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:Sumiko Morita Kofun]] [[Category:new pages]] [[sn:Sumiko Morita Kofun]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Sumiko Morita Kofun]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Sumiko Morita Kofun]][[Category:qq]]

Kofun|lat_deg=35|lat_min=43|lat_sec=18.3|lon_deg=140|lon_min=16|lon_sec=46.0|mark=方墳.svg|marksize=25|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location within Chiba Prefecture}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|43|18.3|N|140|16|46.0|E}}}} }} '''Sumikomorida Kofun''' (墨小盛田古墳, also pronounced as Sumiko Morita Kofun{{Cite web|和書|author=Shisui Town Hall|url=https://www.town.shisui.chiba.jp/navi/mobile/id/374.html|title=Sumikomorida Kofun|work=Shisui Tourism Navi|accessdate=2020-08-20}}) is a {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳}} located in {{ill|Shisui, Chiba|ja|酒々井町}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}}, Japan. == Overview == The burial mound measures approximately 30 meters north-south, 23 meters east-west, and 3 meters in height. Rectangular burial mounds are a characteristic type of kofun found in the {{ill|Shimousa Province|ja|下総国}} region during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. In November 1986, during the construction of the {{ill|Shisui Comprehensive Park|ja|酒々井総合公園}}, a one-month excavation was conducted. The mound features a 2-meter-wide surrounding moat and a protruding section. The main burial chamber, located at the center of the southern side of the mound, was identified as a {{ill|horizontal stone burial chamber|ja|横口式石槨}}. Based on the {{ill|sue ware|ja|須恵器}} found on the terrace section, the kofun is believed to have been constructed in the late 7th to early 8th century. Restoration work was carried out in February 1987. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * Shisui Town and Inba District Cultural Property Center. _Excavation Report on the Shisui Town Comprehensive Park Site: Onoue Hirohata Ruins and Sumikomorida Kofun._ 1987. == Related Topics == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧}} {{japanese-history-stub}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kofun in Chiba Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Rectangular Kofun]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:History of Shisui, Chiba]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:attempting to force efresh]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:bump 1-20 (2)]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Rectangular Kofun]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Sumiko Morita Kofun]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Rectangular Kofun]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] {{Kofun navbar}} [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Sumiko Morita Kofun]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:Sumiko Morita Kofun]] [[Category:new pages]] [[sn:Sumiko Morita Kofun]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Sumiko Morita Kofun]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Sumiko Morita Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:横口式石槨]][[sn:Sumiko Morita Kofun]][[Category:横口式石槨]]

Kofun|lat_deg=35|lat_min=43|lat_sec=18.3|lon_deg=140|lon_min=16|lon_sec=46.0|mark=方墳.svg|marksize=25|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location within Chiba Prefecture}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|43|18.3|N|140|16|46.0|E}}}} }} '''Sumikomorida Kofun''' (墨小盛田古墳, also pronounced as Sumiko Morita Kofun{{Cite web|和書|author=Shisui Town Hall|url=https://www.town.shisui.chiba.jp/navi/mobile/id/374.html|title=Sumikomorida Kofun|work=Shisui Tourism Navi|accessdate=2020-08-20}}) is a {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳}} located in {{ill|Shisui, Chiba|ja|酒々井町}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}}, Japan. == Overview == The burial mound measures approximately 30 meters north-south, 23 meters east-west, and 3 meters in height. Rectangular burial mounds are a characteristic type of kofun found in the {{ill|Shimousa Province|ja|下総国}} region during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. In November 1986, during the construction of the {{ill|Shisui Comprehensive Park|ja|酒々井総合公園}}, a one-month excavation was conducted. The mound features a 2-meter-wide surrounding moat and a protruding section. The main burial chamber, located at the center of the southern side of the mound, was identified as a {{ill|horizontal stone burial chamber|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}}. Based on the {{ill|sue ware|ja|須恵器}} found on the terrace section, the kofun is believed to have been constructed in the late 7th to early 8th century. Restoration work was carried out in February 1987. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * Shisui Town and Inba District Cultural Property Center. _Excavation Report on the Shisui Town {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}}, {{ill|Inba District, Chiba|ja|印旛郡}}, {{ill|Shisui, Chiba|ja|酒々井町}}, {{ill|Sumi (Shisui)|ja|墨}} Aza Morita 36-1 |史跡 = None |coordinates = {{coordinates|35|43|18.3|N|140|16|46.0|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} |形状 = Rectangular burial mound |規模 = 30m (north-south) × 23m (east-west) |築造年代 = Late 7th century to 8th century |地図2 = {{location map|Japan Chiba|label=Sumikomorida
Kofun|lat_deg=35|lat_min=43|lat_sec=18.3|lon_deg=140|lon_min=16|lon_sec=46.0|mark=方墳.svg|marksize=25|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location within Chiba Prefecture}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|43|18.3|N|140|16|46.0|E}}}} }} '''Sumikomorida Kofun''' (墨小盛田古墳, also pronounced as Sumiko Morita Kofun{{Cite web|和書|author=Shisui Town Hall|url=https://www.town.shisui.chiba.jp/navi/mobile/id/374.html|title=Sumikomorida Kofun|work=Shisui Tourism Navi|accessdate=2020-08-20}}) is a {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳}} located in {{ill|Shisui, Chiba|ja|酒々井町}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}}, Japan. == Overview == The burial mound measures approximately 30 meters north-south, 23 meters east-west, and 3 meters in height. Rectangular burial mounds are a characteristic type of kofun found in the {{ill|Shimousa Province|ja|下総国}} region during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. In November 1986, during the construction of the {{ill|Shisui Comprehensive Park|ja|酒々井総合公園}}, a one-month excavation was conducted. The mound features a 2-meter-wide surrounding moat and a protruding section. The main burial chamber, located at the center of the southern side of the mound, was identified as a {{ill|horizontal stone burial chamber|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}}. Based on the {{ill|sue ware|ja|須恵器}} found on the terrace section, the kofun is believed to have been constructed in the late 7th to early 8th century. Restoration work was carried out in February 1987. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * Shisui Town and Inba District Cultural Property Center. _Excavation Report on the Shisui Town Comprehensive Park Site: Onoue Hirohata Ruins and Sumikomorida Kofun._ 1987. == Related Topics == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧}} {{japanese-history-stub}} Kofun|lat_deg=35|lat_min=43|lat_sec=18.3|lon_deg=140|lon_min=16|lon_sec=46.0|mark=方墳.svg|marksize=25|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location within Chiba Prefecture}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|43|18.3|N|140|16|46.0|E}}}} }} '''Sumikomorida Kofun''' (墨小盛田古墳, also pronounced as Sumiko Morita Kofun{{Cite web|和書|author=Shisui Town Hall|url=https://www.town.shisui.chiba.jp/navi/mobile/id/374.html|title=Sumikomorida Kofun|work=Shisui Tourism Navi|accessdate=2020-08-20}}) is a {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun}} located in {{ill|Shisui, Chiba|ja|酒々井町}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}}, Japan. == Overview == The burial mound measures approximately 30 meters north-south, 23 meters east-west, and 3 meters in height. Rectangular burial mounds are a characteristic type of kofun found in the {{ill|Shimousa Province|ja|下総国}} region during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. In November 1986, during the construction of the {{ill|Shisui Comprehensive Park|ja|酒々井総合公園}}, a one-month excavation was conducted. The mound features a 2-meter-wide surrounding moat and a protruding section. The main burial chamber, located at the center of the southern side of the mound, was identified as a {{ill|horizontal stone burial chamber|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}}. Based on the {{ill|sue ware|ja|須恵器}} found on the terrace section, the kofun is believed to have been constructed in the late 7th to early 8th century. Restoration work was carried out in February 1987. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * Shisui Town and Inba District Cultural Property Center. _Excavation Report on the Shisui Town Comprehensive Park Site: Onoue Hirohata Ruins and Sumikomorida Kofun._ 1987. == See Also == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧}} {{japanese-history-stub}} [[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Sumiko Morita Kofun}} [[ja:墨小盛田古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site |name = Sumikomorida Kofun |image = [[File:Sumikomorida Kofun.JPG|250px]] |imageキャプション = Burial mound |所在地 = {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}}, {{ill|Inba District, Chiba|ja|印旛郡}}, {{ill|Shisui, Chiba|ja|酒々井町}}, {{ill|Sumi (Shisui)|ja|墨}} Aza Morita 36-1 |史跡 = None |coordinates = {{coordinates|35|43|18.3|N|140|16|46.0|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} |形状 = Rectangular burial mound |規模 = 30m (north-south) × 23m (east-west) |築造年代 = Late 7th century to 8th century |地図2 = {{location map|Japan Chiba|label=Sumikomorida
Kofun|lat_deg=35|lat_min=43|lat_sec=18.3|lon_deg=140|lon_min=16|lon_sec=46.0|mark=方墳.svg|marksize=25|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location within Chiba Prefecture}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|43|18.3|N|140|16|46.0|E}}}} }} '''Sumikomorida Kofun''' (墨小盛田古墳, also pronounced as Sumiko Morita Kofun{{Cite web|和書|author=Shisui Town Hall|url=https://www.town.shisui.chiba.jp/navi/mobile/id/374.html|title=Sumikomorida Kofun|work=Shisui Tourism Navi|accessdate=2020-08-20}}) is a {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun}} located in {{ill|Shisui, Chiba|ja|酒々井町}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}}, Japan. == Overview == The burial mound measures approximately 30 meters north-south, 23 meters east-west, and 3 meters in height. Rectangular burial mounds are a characteristic type of kofun found in the {{ill|Shimousa Province|ja|下総国}} region during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. In November 1986, during the construction of the {{ill|Shisui Comprehensive Park|ja|酒々井総合公園}}, a one-month excavation was conducted. The mound features a 2-meter-wide surrounding moat and a protruding section. The main burial chamber, located at the center of the southern side of the mound, was identified as a {{ill|horizontal stone burial chamber|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}}. Based on the {{ill|sue ware|ja|須恵器}} found on the terrace section, the kofun is believed to have been constructed in the late 7th to early 8th century. Restoration work was carried out in February 1987. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * Shisui Town and Inba District Cultural Property Center. _Excavation Report on the Shisui Town Comprehensive Park Site: Onoue Hirohata Ruins and Sumikomorida Kofun._ 1987. == See Also == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧}} {{japanese-history-stub}} [[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Sumiko Morita Kofun}} [[ja:墨小盛田古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site |name = Sumikomorida Kofun |image = [[File:Sumikomorida Kofun.JPG|250px]] |imageキャプション = Burial mound |所在地 = {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}}, {{ill|Inba District, Chiba|ja|印旛郡}}, {{ill|Shisui, Chiba|ja|酒々井町}}, {{ill|Sumi (Shisui)|ja|墨}} Aza Morita 36-1 |史跡 = None |coordinates = {{coordinates|35|43|18.3|N|140|16|46.0|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} |形状 = Rectangular burial mound |規模 = 30m (north-south) × 23m (east-west) |築造年代 = Late 7th century to 8th century |地図2 = {{location map|Japan Chiba|label=Sumikomorida
Kofun|lat_deg=35|lat_min=43|lat_sec=18.3|lon_deg=140|lon_min=16|lon_sec=46.0|mark=方墳.svg|marksize=25|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location within Chiba Prefecture}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|43|18.3|N|140|16|46.0|E}}}} }} '''Sumikomorida Kofun''' (墨小盛田古墳, also pronounced as Sumiko Morita Kofun{{Cite web|和書|author=Shisui Town Hall|url=https://www.town.shisui.chiba.jp/navi/mobile/id/374.html|title=Sumikomorida Kofun|work=Shisui Tourism Navi|accessdate=2020-08-20}}) is a {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun}} located in {{ill|Shisui, Chiba|ja|酒々井町}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}}, Japan. == Overview == The burial mound measures approximately 30 meters north-south, 23 meters east-west, and 3 meters in height. Rectangular burial mounds are a characteristic type of kofun found in the {{ill|Shimousa Province|ja|下総国}} region during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. In November 1986, during the construction of the {{ill|Shisui Comprehensive Park|ja|酒々井総合公園}}, a one-month excavation was conducted. The mound features a 2-meter-wide surrounding moat and a protruding section. The main burial chamber, located at the center of the southern side of the mound, was identified as a {{ill|horizontal stone burial chamber|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}}. Based on the {{ill|sue ware|ja|須恵器}} found on the terrace section, the kofun is believed to have been constructed in the late 7th to early 8th century. Restoration work was carried out in February 1987. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * Shisui Town and Inba District Cultural Property Center. _Excavation Report on the Shisui Town Comprehensive Park Site: Onoue Hirohata Ruins and Sumikomorida Kofun._ 1987. == See Also == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧}} {{japanese-history-stub}} |imageキャプション = Kofun |所在地 = {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}}, {{ill|Inba District, Chiba|ja|印旛郡}}, {{ill|Shisui, Chiba|ja|酒々井町}}, {{ill|Sumi (Shisui)|ja|墨}} Aza Morita 36-1 |史跡 = None |coordinates = {{coordinates|35|43|18.3|N|140|16|46.0|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} |形状 = Rectangular burial mound |規模 = 30m (north-south) × 23m (east-west) |築造年代 = Late 7th century to 8th century |地図2 = {{location map|Japan Chiba|label=Sumikomorida
Kofun|lat_deg=35|lat_min=43|lat_sec=18.3|lon_deg=140|lon_min=16|lon_sec=46.0|mark=方墳.svg|marksize=25|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location within Chiba Prefecture}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|43|18.3|N|140|16|46.0|E}}}} }} '''Sumikomorida Kofun''' (墨小盛田古墳, also pronounced as Sumiko Morita Kofun{{Cite web|和書|author=Shisui Town Hall|url=https://www.town.shisui.chiba.jp/navi/mobile/id/374.html|title=Sumikomorida Kofun|work=Shisui Tourism Navi|accessdate=2020-08-20}}) is a {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun}} located in {{ill|Shisui, Chiba|ja|酒々井町}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}}, Japan. == Overview == The burial mound measures approximately 30 meters north-south, 23 meters east-west, and 3 meters in height. Rectangular burial mounds are a characteristic type of kofun found in the {{ill|Shimousa Province|ja|下総国}} region during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. In November 1986, during the construction of the {{ill|Shisui Comprehensive Park|ja|酒々井総合公園}}, a one-month excavation was conducted. The mound features a 2-meter-wide surrounding moat and a protruding section. The main burial chamber, located at the center of the southern side of the mound, was identified as a {{ill|horizontal stone burial chamber|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}}. Based on the {{ill|sue ware|ja|須恵器}} found on the terrace section, the kofun is believed to have been constructed in the late 7th to early 8th century. Restoration work was carried out in February 1987. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * Shisui Town and Inba District Cultural Property Center. _Excavation Report on the Shisui Town Comprehensive Park Site: Onoue Hirohata Ruins and Sumikomorida Kofun._ 1987. == See Also == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧}} {{japanese-history-stub}} |imageキャプション = Kofun |所在地 = {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}}, {{ill|Inba District, Chiba|ja|印旛郡}}, {{ill|Shisui, Chiba|ja|酒々井町}}, {{ill|Sumi (Shisui)|ja|墨}} Aza Morita 36-1 |史跡 = None |coordinates = {{coordinates|35|43|18.3|N|140|16|46.0|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} |形状 = Rectangular Kofun |規模 = 30m (north-south) × 23m (east-west) |築造年代 = Late 7th century to 8th century |地図2 = {{location map|Japan Chiba|label=Sumikomorida
Kofun|lat_deg=35|lat_min=43|lat_sec=18.3|lon_deg=140|lon_min=16|lon_sec=46.0|mark=方墳.svg|marksize=25|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location within Chiba Prefecture}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|43|18.3|N|140|16|46.0|E}}}} }} '''Sumikomorida Kofun''' (墨小盛田古墳, also pronounced as Sumiko Morita Kofun{{Cite web|和書|author=Shisui Town Hall|url=https://www.town.shisui.chiba.jp/navi/mobile/id/374.html|title=Sumikomorida Kofun|work=Shisui Tourism Navi|accessdate=2020-08-20}}) is a {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun}} located in {{ill|Shisui, Chiba|ja|酒々井町}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}}, Japan. == Overview == The Kofun measures approximately 30 meters north-south, 23 meters east-west, and 3 meters in height. Rectangular Kofuns are a characteristic type of kofun found in the {{ill|Shimousa Province|ja|下総国}} region during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. In November 1986, during the construction of the {{ill|Shisui Comprehensive Park|ja|酒々井総合公園}}, a one-month excavation was conducted. The mound features a 2-meter-wide surrounding moat and a protruding section. The main burial chamber, located at the center of the southern side of the mound, was identified as a {{ill|horizontal stone burial chamber|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}}. Based on the {{ill|sue ware|ja|須恵器}} found on the terrace section, the kofun is believed to have been constructed in the late 7th to early 8th century. Restoration work was carried out in February 1987. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * Shisui Town and Inba District Cultural Property Center. _Excavation Report on the Shisui Town Comprehensive Park Site: Onoue Hirohata Ruins and Sumikomorida Kofun._ 1987. == See Also == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧}} {{japanese-history-stub}} [[File:Sumikomorida Kofun.JPG|250px]] |imageキャプション = Kofun |所在地 = {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}}, {{ill|Inba District, Chiba|ja|印旛郡}}, {{ill|Shisui, Chiba|ja|酒々井町}}, {{ill|Sumi (Shisui)|ja|墨}} Aza Morita 36-1 |史跡 = None |coordinates = {{coordinates|35|43|18.3|N|140|16|46.0|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} |形状 = Rectangular Kofun |規模 = 30m (north-south) × 23m (east-west) |築造年代 = Late 7th century to 8th century |地図2 = {{location map|Japan Chiba|label=Sumikomorida
Kofun|lat_deg=35|lat_min=43|lat_sec=18.3|lon_deg=140|lon_min=16|lon_sec=46.0|mark=方墳.svg|marksize=25|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location within Chiba Prefecture}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|43|18.3|N|140|16|46.0|E}}}} }} '''Sumikomorida Kofun''' (墨小盛田古墳, also pronounced as Sumiko Morita Kofun{{Cite web|和書|author=Shisui Town Hall|url=https://www.town.shisui.chiba.jp/navi/mobile/id/374.html|title=Sumikomorida Kofun|work=Shisui Tourism Navi|accessdate=2020-08-20}}) is a {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun}} located in {{ill|Shisui, Chiba|ja|酒々井町}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}}, Japan. == Overview == The Kofun measures approximately 30 meters north-south, 23 meters east-west, and 3 meters in height. Rectangular Kofuns are a characteristic type of kofun found in the {{ill|Shimousa Province|ja|下総国}} region during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. In November 1986, during the construction of the {{ill|Shisui Comprehensive Park|ja|酒々井総合公園}}, a one-month excavation was conducted. The mound features a 2-meter-wide surrounding moat and a protruding section. The main burial chamber, located at the center of the southern side of the mound, was identified as a {{ill|horizontal stone burial chamber|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}}. Based on the {{ill|sue ware|ja|須恵器}} found on the terrace section, the kofun is believed to have been constructed in the late 7th to early 8th century. Restoration work was carried out in February 1987. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * Shisui Town and Inba District Cultural Property Center. _Excavation Report on the Shisui Town Comprehensive Park Site: Onoue Hirohata Ruins and Sumikomorida Kofun._ 1987. == See Also == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧}} {{japanese-history-stub}} Morita 36-1 |史跡 = None |coordinates = {{coordinates|35|43|18.3|N|140|16|46.0|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} |形状 = Rectangular Kofun |規模 = 30m (north-south) × 23m (east-west) |築造年代 = Late 7th century to 8th century |地図2 = {{location map|Japan Chiba|label=Sumikomorida
Kofun|lat_deg=35|lat_min=43|lat_sec=18.3|lon_deg=140|lon_min=16|lon_sec=46.0|mark=方墳.svg|marksize=25|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location within Chiba Prefecture}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|43|18.3|N|140|16|46.0|E}}}} }} '''Sumikomorida Kofun''' (墨小盛田古墳, also pronounced as Sumiko Morita Kofun{{Cite web|和書|author=Shisui Town Hall|url=https://www.town.shisui.chiba.jp/navi/mobile/id/374.html|title=Sumikomorida Kofun|work=Shisui Tourism Navi|accessdate=2020-08-20}}) is a {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun}} located in {{ill|Shisui, Chiba|ja|酒々井町}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}}, Japan. == Overview == The Kofun measures approximately 30 meters north-south, 23 meters east-west, and 3 meters in height. Rectangular Kofuns are a characteristic type of kofun found in the {{ill|Shimousa Province|ja|下総国}} region during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. In November 1986, during the construction of the {{ill|Shisui Comprehensive Park|ja|酒々井総合公園}}, a one-month excavation was conducted. The mound features a 2-meter-wide surrounding moat and a protruding section. The main burial chamber, located at the center of the southern side of the mound, was identified as a {{ill|horizontal stone burial chamber|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}}. Based on the {{ill|sue ware|ja|須恵器}} found on the terrace section, the kofun is believed to have been constructed in the late 7th to early 8th century. Restoration work was carried out in February 1987. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * Shisui Town and Inba District Cultural Property Center. _Excavation Report on the Shisui Town Comprehensive Park Site: Onoue Hirohata Ruins and Sumikomorida Kofun._ 1987. == See Also == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧}} {{japanese-history-stub}} Comprehensive Park Site: Onoue Hirohata Ruins and Sumikomorida Kofun._ 1987. == See Also == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧}} {{japanese-history-stub}} Sumikomorida Kofun |image = [[File:Sumikomorida Kofun.JPG|250px]] |imageキャプション = Kofun |所在地 = {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}}, {{ill|Inba District, Chiba|ja|印旛郡}}, {{ill|Shisui, Chiba|ja|酒々井町}}, {{ill|Sumi (Shisui)|ja|墨}} Aza Morita 36-1 |史跡 = None |coordinates = {{coordinates|35|43|18.3|N|140|16|46.0|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} |type = Rectangular Kofun |規模 = 30m (north-south) × 23m (east-west) |築造年代 = Late 7th century to 8th century |地図2 = {{location map|Japan Chiba|label=Sumikomorida
Kofun|lat_deg=35|lat_min=43|lat_sec=18.3|lon_deg=140|lon_min=16|lon_sec=46.0|mark=方墳.svg|marksize=25|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location within Chiba Prefecture}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|43|18.3|N|140|16|46.0|E}}}} }} '''Sumikomorida Kofun''' (墨小盛田古墳, also pronounced as Sumiko Morita Kofun{{Cite web|和書|author=Shisui Town Hall|url=https://www.town.shisui.chiba.jp/navi/mobile/id/374.html|title=Sumikomorida Kofun|work=Shisui Tourism Navi|accessdate=2020-08-20}}) is a {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun}} located in {{ill|Shisui, Chiba|ja|酒々井町}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}}, Japan. == Overview == The Kofun measures approximately 30 meters north-south, 23 meters east-west, and 3 meters in height. Rectangular Kofuns are a characteristic type of kofun found in the {{ill|Shimousa Province|ja|下総国}} region during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. In November 1986, during the construction of the {{ill|Shisui Comprehensive Park|ja|酒々井総合公園}}, a one-month excavation was conducted. The mound features a 2-meter-wide surrounding moat and a protruding section. The main burial chamber, located at the center of the southern side of the mound, was identified as a {{ill|horizontal stone burial chamber|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}}. Based on the {{ill|sue ware|ja|須恵器}} found on the terrace section, the kofun is believed to have been constructed in the late 7th to early 8th century. Restoration work was carried out in February 1987. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * Shisui Town and Inba District Cultural Property Center. _Excavation Report on the Shisui Town Comprehensive Park Site: Onoue Hirohata Ruins and Sumikomorida Kofun._ 1987. == See Also == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧}} {{japanese-history-stub}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} [[Category:11]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Sumiko Morita Kofun}} [[ja:墨小盛田古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Rectangular Kofun|ja|長方形墳|en}} |name = Sumikomorida Kofun |image = [[File:Sumikomorida Kofun.JPG|250px]] |imageキャプション = Kofun |所在地 = {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}}, {{ill|Inba District, Chiba|ja|印旛郡}}, {{ill|Shisui, Chiba|ja|酒々井町}}, {{ill|Sumi (Shisui)|ja|墨}} Aza Morita 36-1 |史跡 = None |coordinates = {{coordinates|35|43|18.3|N|140|16|46.0|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} |type = Rectangular Kofun |規模 = 30m (north-south) × 23m (east-west) |築造年代 = Late 7th century to 8th century |地図2 = {{location map|Japan Chiba|label=Sumikomorida
Kofun|lat_deg=35|lat_min=43|lat_sec=18.3|lon_deg=140|lon_min=16|lon_sec=46.0|mark=方墳.svg|marksize=25|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location within Chiba Prefecture}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|43|18.3|N|140|16|46.0|E}}}} }} '''Sumikomorida Kofun''' (墨小盛田古墳, also pronounced as Sumiko Morita Kofun{{Cite web|和書|author=Shisui Town Hall|url=https://www.town.shisui.chiba.jp/navi/mobile/id/374.html|title=Sumikomorida Kofun|work=Shisui Tourism Navi|accessdate=2020-08-20}}) is a {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun}} located in {{ill|Shisui, Chiba|ja|酒々井町}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}}, Japan. == Overview == The Kofun measures approximately 30 meters north-south, 23 meters east-west, and 3 meters in height. Rectangular Kofuns are a characteristic type of kofun found in the {{ill|Shimousa Province|ja|下総国}} region during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. In November 1986, during the construction of the {{ill|Shisui Comprehensive Park|ja|酒々井総合公園}}, a one-month excavation was conducted. The mound features a 2-meter-wide surrounding moat and a protruding section. The main burial chamber, located at the center of the southern side of the mound, was identified as a {{ill|horizontal stone burial chamber|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}}. Based on the {{ill|sue ware|ja|須恵器}} found on the terrace section, the kofun is believed to have been constructed in the late 7th to early 8th century. Restoration work was carried out in February 1987. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * Shisui Town and Inba District Cultural Property Center. _Excavation Report on the Shisui Town Comprehensive Park Site: Onoue Hirohata Ruins and Sumikomorida Kofun._ 1987. == See Also! == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧}} {{japanese-history-stub}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:11]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kofun in Chiba Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:11]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Rectangular Kofun]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:11]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:History of Shisui, Chiba]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:11]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:11]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:11]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Rectangular Kofun]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:11]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Sumiko Morita Kofun]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:11]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Rectangular Kofun]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Sumiko Morita Kofun]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:Sumiko Morita Kofun]] [[sn:Sumiko Morita Kofun]][[Category:11]][[sn:Sumiko Morita Kofun]][[Category:11]][[sn:Sumiko Morita Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[Category:横口式石槨]][[sn:Sumiko Morita Kofun]][[Category:横口式石槨]] [[sn:Sumiko Morita Kofun]][[sn:Sumiko Morita Kofun]][[sn:Sumiko Morita Kofun]][[sn:Sumiko Morita Kofun]][[sn:Sumiko Morita Kofun]][[sn:Sumiko Morita Kofun]][[sn:Sumiko Morita Kofun]][[Category:New]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Sumiko Morita Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Sumiko Morita Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:return the see also]] {{Kofun navbar}}

Sumikomorida Kofun |image = [[File:Sumikomorida Kofun.JPG|250px]] |imageキャプション = Kofun |所在地 = {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}}, {{ill|Inba District, Chiba|ja|印旛郡}}, {{ill|Shisui, Chiba|ja|酒々井町}}, {{ill|Sumi (Shisui)|ja|墨}} Aza Morita 36-1 |史跡 = None |coordinates = {{coordinates|35|43|18.3|N|140|16|46.0|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} |type = Rectangular Kofun |規模 = 30m (north-south) × 23m (east-west) |築造年代 = Late 7th century to 8th century |地図2 = {{location map|Japan Chiba|label=Sumikomorida
Kofun|lat_deg=35|lat_min=43|lat_sec=18.3|lon_deg=140|lon_min=16|lon_sec=46.0|mark=方墳.svg|marksize=25|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location within Chiba Prefecture}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|43|18.3|N|140|16|46.0|E}}}} }} '''Sumikomorida Kofun''' (墨小盛田古墳, also pronounced as Sumiko Morita Kofun{{Cite web|和書|author=Shisui Town Hall|url=https://www.town.shisui.chiba.jp/navi/mobile/id/374.html|title=Sumikomorida Kofun|work=Shisui Tourism Navi|accessdate=2020-08-20}}) is a {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun}} located in {{ill|Shisui, Chiba|ja|酒々井町}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}}, Japan. == Overview == The Kofun measures approximately 30 meters north-south, 23 meters east-west, and 3 meters in height. Rectangular Kofuns are a characteristic type of kofun found in the {{ill|Shimousa Province|ja|下総国}} region during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. In November 1986, during the construction of the {{ill|Shisui Comprehensive Park|ja|酒々井総合公園}}, a one-month excavation was conducted. The mound features a 2-meter-wide surrounding moat and a protruding section. The main burial chamber, located at the center of the southern side of the mound, was identified as a {{ill|horizontal stone burial chamber|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}}. Based on the {{ill|sue ware|ja|須恵器}} found on the terrace section, the kofun is believed to have been constructed in the late 7th to early 8th century. Restoration work was carried out in February 1987. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * Shisui Town and Inba District Cultural Property Center. _Excavation Report on the Shisui Town Comprehensive Park Site: Onoue Hirohata Ruins and Sumikomorida Kofun._ 1987. == See Also == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧}} {{japanese-history-stub}} Kofun|lat_deg=35|lat_min=43|lat_sec=18.3|lon_deg=140|lon_min=16|lon_sec=46.0|mark=方墳.svg|marksize=25|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location within Chiba Prefecture}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|43|18.3|N|140|16|46.0|E}}}} }} '''Sumikomorida Kofun''' (墨小盛田古墳, also pronounced as Sumiko Morita Kofun{{Cite web|和書|author=Shisui Town Hall|url=https://www.town.shisui.chiba.jp/navi/mobile/id/374.html|title=Sumikomorida Kofun|work=Shisui Tourism Navi|accessdate=2020-08-20}}) is a {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun}} located in {{ill|Shisui, Chiba|ja|酒々井町}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}}, Japan. == Overview == The Kofun measures approximately 30 meters north-south, 23 meters east-west, and 3 meters in height. Rectangular Kofuns are a characteristic type of kofun found in the {{ill|Shimousa Province|ja|下総国}} region during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. In November 1986, during the construction of the {{ill|Shisui Comprehensive Park|ja|酒々井総合公園}}, a one-month excavation was conducted. The mound features a 2-meter-wide surrounding moat and a protruding section. The main burial chamber, located at the center of the southern side of the mound, was identified as a {{ill|horizontal stone burial chamber|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}}. Based on the {{ill|sue ware|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}} found on the terrace section, the kofun is believed to have been constructed in the late 7th to early 8th century. Restoration work was carried out in February 1987. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * Shisui Town and Inba District Cultural Property Center. _Excavation Report on the Shisui Town Comprehensive Park Site: Onoue Hirohata Ruins and Sumikomorida Kofun._ 1987. == See Also == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧}} {{japanese-history-stub}} Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{ill|Sumi (Shisui)|ja|墨}} Aza Morita 36-1 |史跡 = None |coordinates = {{coordinates|35|43|18.3|N|140|16|46.0|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} |type = Rectangular Kofun |規模 = 30m (north-south) × 23m (east-west) |築造年代 = Late 7th century to 8th century |地図2 = {{location map|Japan Chiba|label=Sumikomorida
Kofun|lat_deg=35|lat_min=43|lat_sec=18.3|lon_deg=140|lon_min=16|lon_sec=46.0|mark=方墳.svg|marksize=25|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location within Chiba Prefecture}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|43|18.3|N|140|16|46.0|E}}}} }} '''Sumikomorida Kofun''' (墨小盛田古墳, also pronounced as Sumiko Morita Kofun{{Cite web|和書|author=Shisui Town Hall|url=https://www.town.shisui.chiba.jp/navi/mobile/id/374.html|title=Sumikomorida Kofun|work=Shisui Tourism Navi|accessdate=2020-08-20}}) is a {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}} located in {{ill|Shisui, Chiba|ja|酒々井町}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}}, Japan. == Overview == The Kofun measures approximately 30 meters north-south, 23 meters east-west, and 3 meters in height. Rectangular Kofuns are a characteristic type of kofun found in the {{ill|Shimousa Province|ja|下総国}} region during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. In November 1986, during the construction of the {{ill|Shisui Comprehensive Park|ja|酒々井総合公園}}, a one-month excavation was conducted. The mound features a 2-meter-wide surrounding moat and a protruding section. The main burial chamber, located at the center of the southern side of the mound, was identified as a {{ill|horizontal stone burial chamber|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}}. Based on the {{ill|sue ware|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}} found on the terrace section, the kofun is believed to have been constructed in the late 7th to early 8th century. Restoration work was carried out in February 1987. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * Shisui Town and Inba District Cultural Property Center. _Excavation Report on the Shisui Town Comprehensive Park Site: Onoue Hirohata Ruins and Sumikomorida Kofun._ 1987. == See Also == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧}} {{japanese-history-stub}} [[Category:11]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Sumiko Morita Kofun}} [[ja:墨小盛田古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Rectangular Kofun|ja|長方形墳|en}} |name = Sumikomorida Kofun |image = [[File:Sumikomorida Kofun.JPG|250px]] |imageキャプション = Kofun |所在地 = {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}}, {{ill|Inba District, Chiba|ja|印旛郡}}, {{ill|Shisui, Chiba|ja|酒々井町}}, {{ill|Sumi (Shisui)|ja|墨}} Aza Morita 36-1 |史跡 = None |coordinates = {{coordinates|35|43|18.3|N|140|16|46.0|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} |type = Rectangular Kofun |規模 = 30m (north-south) × 23m (east-west) |築造年代 = Late 7th century to 8th century |地図2 = {{location map|Japan Chiba|label=Sumikomorida
Kofun|lat_deg=35|lat_min=43|lat_sec=18.3|lon_deg=140|lon_min=16|lon_sec=46.0|mark=方墳.svg|marksize=25|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location within Chiba Prefecture}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|43|18.3|N|140|16|46.0|E}}}} }} '''Sumikomorida Kofun''' (墨小盛田古墳, also pronounced as Sumiko Morita Kofun{{Cite web|和書|author=Shisui Town Hall|url=https://www.town.shisui.chiba.jp/navi/mobile/id/374.html|title=Sumikomorida Kofun|work=Shisui Tourism Navi|accessdate=2020-08-20}}) is a {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}} located in {{ill|Shisui, Chiba|ja|酒々井町}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}}, Japan. == Overview == The Kofun measures approximately 30 meters north-south, 23 meters east-west, and 3 meters in height. Rectangular Kofuns are a characteristic type of kofun found in the {{ill|Shimousa Province|ja|下総国}} region during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. In November 1986, during the construction of the {{ill|Shisui Comprehensive Park|ja|酒々井総合公園}}, a one-month excavation was conducted. The mound features a 2-meter-wide surrounding moat and a protruding section. The main burial chamber, located at the center of the southern side of the mound, was identified as a {{ill|horizontal stone burial chamber|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}}. Based on the {{ill|sue ware|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}} found on the terrace section, the kofun is believed to have been constructed in the late 7th to early 8th century. Restoration work was carried out in February 1987. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * Shisui Town and Inba District Cultural Property Center. _Excavation Report on the Shisui Town Comprehensive Park Site: Onoue Hirohata Ruins and Sumikomorida Kofun._ 1987. == See Also == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧}} {{japanese-history-stub}} [[Category:11]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Sumiko Morita Kofun}} [[ja:墨小盛田古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Rectangular Kofun|ja|長方形墳|en}} |name = Sumikomorida Kofun |image = [[File:Sumikomorida Kofun.JPG|250px]] |imageキャプション = Kofun |location = {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}}, {{ill|Inba District, Chiba|ja|印旛郡}}, {{ill|Shisui, Chiba|ja|酒々井町}}, {{ill|Sumi (Shisui)|ja|墨}} Aza Morita 36-1 |史跡 = None |coordinates = {{coordinates|35|43|18.3|N|140|16|46.0|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} |type = Rectangular Kofun |area = 30m (north-south) × 23m (east-west) |築造年代 = Late 7th century to 8th century |地図2 = {{location map|Japan Chiba|label=Sumikomorida
Kofun|lat_deg=35|lat_min=43|lat_sec=18.3|lon_deg=140|lon_min=16|lon_sec=46.0|mark=方墳.svg|marksize=25|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location within Chiba Prefecture}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|43|18.3|N|140|16|46.0|E}}}} }} '''Sumikomorida Kofun''' (墨小盛田古墳, also pronounced as Sumiko Morita Kofun{{Cite web|和書|author=Shisui Town Hall|url=https://www.town.shisui.chiba.jp/navi/mobile/id/374.html|title=Sumikomorida Kofun|work=Shisui Tourism Navi|accessdate=2020-08-20}}) is a {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}} located in {{ill|Shisui, Chiba|ja|酒々井町}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}}, Japan. == Overview == The Kofun measures approximately 30 meters north-south, 23 meters east-west, and 3 meters in height. Rectangular Kofuns are a characteristic type of kofun found in the {{ill|Shimousa Province|ja|下総国}} region during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. In November 1986, during the construction of the {{ill|Shisui Comprehensive Park|ja|酒々井総合公園}}, a one-month excavation was conducted. The mound features a 2-meter-wide surrounding moat and a protruding section. The main burial chamber, located at the center of the southern side of the mound, was identified as a {{ill|horizontal stone burial chamber|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}}. Based on the {{ill|sue ware|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}} found on the terrace section, the kofun is believed to have been constructed in the late 7th to early 8th century. Restoration work was carried out in February 1987. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * Shisui Town and Inba District Cultural Property Center. _Excavation Report on the Shisui Town Comprehensive Park Site: Onoue Hirohata Ruins and Sumikomorida Kofun._ 1987. == See Also == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧}} {{japanese-history-stub}} [[Category:11]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Sumiko Morita Kofun}} [[ja:墨小盛田古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Rectangular Kofun|ja|長方形墳|en}} |name = Sumikomorida Kofun |image = [[File:Sumikomorida Kofun.JPG|250px]] |imageキャプション = Kofun |location = {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}}, {{ill|Inba District, Chiba|ja|印旛郡}}, {{ill|Shisui, Chiba|ja|酒々井町}}, {{ill|Sumi (Shisui)|ja|墨}} Aza Morita 36-1 |史跡 = None |coordinates = {{coordinates|35|43|18.3|N|140|16|46.0|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} |type = Rectangular Kofun |area = 30m (north-south) × 23m (east-west) |築造年代 = Late 7th century to 8th century |地図2 = {{location map|Japan Chiba|map_dot_label=Sumikomorida
Kofun|lat_deg=35|lat_min=43|lat_sec=18.3|lon_deg=140|lon_min=16|lon_sec=46.0|mark=方墳.svg|marksize=25|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location within Chiba Prefecture}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|43|18.3|N|140|16|46.0|E}}}} }} '''Sumikomorida Kofun''' (墨小盛田古墳, also pronounced as Sumiko Morita Kofun{{Cite web|和書|author=Shisui Town Hall|url=https://www.town.shisui.chiba.jp/navi/mobile/id/374.html|title=Sumikomorida Kofun|work=Shisui Tourism Navi|accessdate=2020-08-20}}) is a {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}} located in {{ill|Shisui, Chiba|ja|酒々井町}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}}, Japan. == Overview == The Kofun measures approximately 30 meters north-south, 23 meters east-west, and 3 meters in height. Rectangular Kofuns are a characteristic type of kofun found in the {{ill|Shimousa Province|ja|下総国}} region during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. In November 1986, during the construction of the {{ill|Shisui Comprehensive Park|ja|酒々井総合公園}}, a one-month excavation was conducted. The mound features a 2-meter-wide surrounding moat and a protruding section. The main burial chamber, located at the center of the southern side of the mound, was identified as a {{ill|horizontal stone burial chamber|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}}. Based on the {{ill|sue ware|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}} found on the terrace section, the kofun is believed to have been constructed in the late 7th to early 8th century. Restoration work was carried out in February 1987. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * Shisui Town and Inba District Cultural Property Center. _Excavation Report on the Shisui Town Comprehensive Park Site: Onoue Hirohata Ruins and Sumikomorida Kofun._ 1987. == See Also == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧}} {{japanese-history-stub}} [[Category:11]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Sumiko Morita Kofun}} [[ja:墨小盛田古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Rectangular Kofun|ja|長方形墳|en}} |name = Sumikomorida Kofun |image = [[File:Sumikomorida Kofun.JPG|250px]] |imageキャプション = Kofun |location = {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}}, {{ill|Inba District, Chiba|ja|印旛郡}}, {{ill|Shisui, Chiba|ja|酒々井町}}, {{ill|Sumi (Shisui)|ja|墨}} Aza Morita 36-1 |史跡 = None |coordinates = {{coordinates|35|43|18.3|N|140|16|46.0|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} |type = Rectangular Kofun |area = 30m (north-south) × 23m (east-west) |築造年代 = Late 7th century to 8th century |地図2 = {{location map|Japan Chiba|map_dot_label=Sumikomorida
Kofun|lat_deg=35|lat_min=43|lat_sec=18.3|lon_deg=140|lon_min=16|lon_sec=46.0|mark=方墳.svg|marksize=25|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location within Chiba Prefecture}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|43|18.3|N|140|16|46.0|E}}}} }} '''Sumikomorida Kofun''' (墨小盛田古墳, also pronounced as Sumiko Morita Kofun{{Cite web|和書|author=Shisui Town Hall|url=https://www.town.shisui.chiba.jp/navi/mobile/id/374.html|title=Sumikomorida Kofun|work=Shisui Tourism Navi|accessdate=2020-08-20}}) is a {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}} located in {{ill|Shisui, Chiba|ja|酒々井町}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}}, Japan. == Overview == The Kofun measures approximately 30 meters north-south, 23 meters east-west, and 3 meters in height. Rectangular Kofuns are a characteristic type of kofun found in the {{ill|Shimousa Province|ja|下総国}} region during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. In November 1986, during the construction of the {{ill|Shisui Comprehensive Park|ja|酒々井総合公園}}, a one-month excavation was conducted. The mound features a 2-meter-wide surrounding moat and a protruding section. The main burial chamber, located at the center of the southern side of the mound, was identified as a {{ill|horizontal stone burial chamber|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}}. Based on the {{ill|sue ware|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}} found on the terrace section, the kofun is believed to have been constructed in the late 7th to early 8th century. Restoration work was carried out in February 1987. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * Shisui Town and Inba District Cultural Property Center. _Excavation Report on the Shisui Town Comprehensive Park Site: Onoue Hirohata Ruins and Sumikomorida Kofun._ 1987. == See Also == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧}} {{japanese-history-stub}}
