[[Category:11]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Sumiko Morita Kofun}} [[ja:墨小盛田古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Rectangular Kofun|ja|長方形墳|en}} |name = Sumikomorida Kofun |image = [[File:Sumikomorida Kofun.JPG|250px]] |imageキャプション = Kofun |location = {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}}, {{ill|Inba District, Chiba|ja|印旛郡}}, {{ill|Shisui, Chiba|ja|酒々井町}}, {{ill|Sumi (Shisui)|ja|墨}} Aza Morita 36-1 |史跡 = None |coordinates = {{coordinates|35|43|18.3|N|140|16|46.0|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} |type = Rectangular Kofun |area = 30m (north-south) × 23m (east-west) |築造年代 = Late 7th century to 8th century |地図2 = {{location map|Japan Chiba|map_dot_label=Sumikomorida
Kofun|lat_deg=35|lat_min=43|lat_sec=18.3|lon_deg=140|lon_min=16|lon_sec=46.0|mark=方墳.svg|marksize=25|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location within Chiba Prefecture}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|43|18.3|N|140|16|46.0|E}}}} }} '''Sumikomorida Kofun''' (墨小盛田古墳, also pronounced as Sumiko Morita Kofun{{Cite web|和書|author=Shisui Town Hall|url=https://www.town.shisui.chiba.jp/navi/mobile/id/374.html|title=Sumikomorida Kofun|work=Shisui Tourism Navi|accessdate=2020-08-20}}) is a {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}} located in {{ill|Shisui, Chiba|ja|酒々井町}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}}, Japan. == Overview == The Kofun measures approximately 30 meters north-south, 23 meters east-west, and 3 meters in height. Rectangular Kofuns are a characteristic type of kofun found in the {{ill|Shimousa Province|ja|下総国}} region during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. In November 1986, during the construction of the {{ill|Shisui Comprehensive Park|ja|酒々井総合公園}}, a one-month excavation was conducted. The mound features a 2-meter-wide surrounding moat and a protruding section. The main burial chamber, located at the center of the southern side of the mound, was identified as a {{ill|horizontal stone burial chamber|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}}. Based on the {{ill|sue ware|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}} found on the terrace section, the kofun is believed to have been constructed in the late 7th to early 8th century. Restoration work was carried out in February 1987. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * Shisui Town and Inba District Cultural Property Center. _Excavation Report on the Shisui Town Comprehensive Park Site: Onoue Hirohata Ruins and Sumikomorida Kofun._ 1987. == See Also == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧}} {{japanese-history-stub}} [[Category:11]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Sumiko Morita Kofun}} [[ja:墨小盛田古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Rectangular Kofun|ja|長方形墳|en}} |name = Sumikomorida Kofun |image = [[File:Sumikomorida Kofun.JPG|250px]] |imageキャプション = Kofun |location = {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}}, {{ill|Inba District, Chiba|ja|印旛郡}}, {{ill|Shisui, Chiba|ja|酒々井町}}, {{ill|Sumi (Shisui)|ja|墨}} Aza Morita 36-1 |史跡 = None |coordinates = {{coordinates|35|43|18.3|N|140|16|46.0|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} |type = Rectangular Kofun |area = 30m (north-south) × 23m (east-west) |築造年代 = Late 7th century to 8th century |地図2 = {{location map|Japan Chiba|map_dot_label=Sumikomorida
Kofun|lat_deg=35|lat_min=43|lat_sec=18.3|lon_deg=140|lon_min=16|lon_sec=46.0|mark=方墳.svg|marksize=25|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location within Chiba Prefecture}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|43|18.3|N|140|16|46.0|E}}}} }} '''Sumikomorida Kofun''' (墨小盛田古墳, also pronounced as Sumiko Morita Kofun{{Cite web|和書|author=Shisui Town Hall|url=https://www.town.shisui.chiba.jp/navi/mobile/id/374.html|title=Sumikomorida Kofun|work=Shisui Tourism Navi|accessdate=2020-08-20}}) is a {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}} located in {{ill|Shisui, Chiba|ja|酒々井町}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}}, Japan. == Overview == The Kofun measures approximately 30 meters north-south, 23 meters east-west, and 3 meters in height. Rectangular Kofuns are a characteristic type of kofun found in the {{ill|Shimousa Province|ja|下総国}} region during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. In November 1986, during the construction of the {{ill|Shisui Comprehensive Park|ja|酒々井総合公園}}, a one-month excavation was conducted. The mound features a 2-meter-wide surrounding moat and a protruding section. The main burial chamber, located at the center of the southern side of the mound, was identified as a {{ill|horizontal stone burial chamber|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}}. Based on the {{ill|sue ware|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}} found on the terrace section, the kofun is believed to have been constructed in the late 7th to early 8th century. Restoration work was carried out in February 1987. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * Shisui Town and Inba District Cultural Property Center. _Excavation Report on the Shisui Town Comprehensive Park Site: Onoue Hirohata Ruins and Sumikomorida Kofun._ 1987. == See Also == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧}} {{japanese-history-stub}} [[Category:11]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Sumiko Morita Kofun}} [[ja:墨小盛田古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Rectangular Kofun|ja|長方形墳|en}} |name = Sumikomorida Kofun |image = [[File:Sumikomorida Kofun.JPG|250px]] |imageキャプション = Kofun |location = {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}}, {{ill|Inba District, Chiba|ja|印旛郡}}, {{ill|Shisui, Chiba|ja|酒々井町}}, {{ill|Sumi (Shisui)|ja|墨}} Aza Morita 36-1 |史跡 = None |coordinates = {{coordinates|35|43|18.3|N|140|16|46.0|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} |type = Rectangular Kofun |area = 30m (north-south) × 23m (east-west) |築造年代 = Late 7th century to 8th century |地図2 = {{location map|Japan Chiba|map_dot_label=Sumikomorida
Kofun|coordinates=35|coordinates=43|coordinates=18.3|lon_deg=140|lon_min=16|lon_sec=46.0|mark=方墳.svg|marksize=25|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location within Chiba Prefecture}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|43|18.3|N|140|16|46.0|E}}}} }} '''Sumikomorida Kofun''' (墨小盛田古墳, also pronounced as Sumiko Morita Kofun{{Cite web|和書|author=Shisui Town Hall|url=https://www.town.shisui.chiba.jp/navi/mobile/id/374.html|title=Sumikomorida Kofun|work=Shisui Tourism Navi|accessdate=2020-08-20}}) is a {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}} located in {{ill|Shisui, Chiba|ja|酒々井町}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}}, Japan. == Overview == The Kofun measures approximately 30 meters north-south, 23 meters east-west, and 3 meters in height. Rectangular Kofuns are a characteristic type of kofun found in the {{ill|Shimousa Province|ja|下総国}} region during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. In November 1986, during the construction of the {{ill|Shisui Comprehensive Park|ja|酒々井総合公園}}, a one-month excavation was conducted. The mound features a 2-meter-wide surrounding moat and a protruding section. The main burial chamber, located at the center of the southern side of the mound, was identified as a {{ill|horizontal stone burial chamber|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}}. Based on the {{ill|sue ware|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}} found on the terrace section, the kofun is believed to have been constructed in the late 7th to early 8th century. Restoration work was carried out in February 1987. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * Shisui Town and Inba District designation1 Center. _Excavation Report on the Shisui Town Comprehensive Park Site: Onoue Hirohata Ruins and Sumikomorida Kofun._ 1987. == See Also == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧}} {{japanese-history-stub}} [[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Sumiko Morita Kofun}} [[ja:墨小盛田古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Rectangular Kofun|ja|長方形墳|en}} |name = Sumikomorida Kofun |image = [[File:Sumikomorida Kofun.JPG|250px]] |imageキャプション = Kofun |location = {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}}, {{ill|Inba District, Chiba|ja|印旛郡}}, {{ill|Shisui, Chiba|ja|酒々井町}}, {{ill|Sumi (Shisui)|ja|墨}} Aza Morita 36-1 |史跡 = None |coordinates = {{coordinates|35|43|18.3|N|140|16|46.0|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} |type = Rectangular Kofun |area = 30m (north-south) × 23m (east-west) |築造年代 = Late 7th century to 8th century |地図2 = {{location map|Japan Chiba|map_dot_label=Sumikomorida
Kofun|coordinates=35|coordinates=43|coordinates=18.3|lon_deg=140|lon_min=16|lon_sec=46.0|mark=方墳.svg|marksize=25|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location within Chiba Prefecture}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|43|18.3|N|140|16|46.0|E}}}} }} '''Sumikomorida Kofun''' (墨小盛田古墳, also pronounced as Sumiko Morita Kofun{{Cite web|和書|author=Shisui Town Hall|url=https://www.town.shisui.chiba.jp/navi/mobile/id/374.html|title=Sumikomorida Kofun|work=Shisui Tourism Navi|accessdate=2020-08-20}}) is a {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}} located in {{ill|Shisui, Chiba|ja|酒々井町}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}}, Japan. == Overview == The Kofun measures approximately 30 meters north-south, 23 meters east-west, and 3 meters in height. Rectangular Kofuns are a characteristic type of kofun found in the {{ill|Shimousa Province|ja|下総国}} region during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. In November 1986, during the construction of the {{ill|Shisui Comprehensive Park|ja|酒々井総合公園}}, a one-month excavation was conducted. The mound features a 2-meter-wide surrounding moat and a protruding section. The main burial chamber, located at the center of the southern side of the mound, was identified as a {{ill|horizontal stone burial chamber|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber}}. Based on the {{ill|sue ware|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}} found on the terrace section, the kofun is believed to have been constructed in the late 7th to early 8th century. Restoration work was carried out in February 1987. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * Shisui Town and Inba District designation1 Center. _Excavation Report on the Shisui Town Comprehensive Park Site: Onoue Hirohata Ruins and Sumikomorida Kofun._ 1987. == See Also == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧}} {{japanese-history-stub}} Kofun|coordinates=35|coordinates=43|coordinates=18.3|lon_deg=140|lon_min=16|lon_sec=46.0|mark=方墳.svg|marksize=25|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location within Chiba Prefecture}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|43|18.3|N|140|16|46.0|E}}}} }} '''Sumikomorida Kofun''' (墨小盛田古墳, also pronounced as Sumiko Morita Kofun{{Cite web|和書|author=Shisui Town Hall|url=https://www.town.shisui.chiba.jp/navi/mobile/id/374.html|title=Sumikomorida Kofun|work=Shisui Tourism Navi|accessdate=2020-08-20}}) is a {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Shisui, Chiba|ja|酒々井町|12=draft}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県|12=draft}}, Japan. == Overview == The Kofun measures approximately 30 meters north-south, 23 meters east-west, and 3 meters in height. Rectangular Kofuns are a characteristic type of kofun found in the {{ill|Shimousa Province|ja|下総国|12=draft}} region during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|12=draft}}. In November 1986, during the construction of the {{ill|Shisui Comprehensive Park|ja|酒々井総合公園|12=draft}}, a one-month excavation was conducted. The mound features a 2-meter-wide surrounding moat and a protruding section. The main burial chamber, located at the center of the southern side of the mound, was identified as a {{ill|horizontal stone burial chamber|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=draft}}. Based on the {{ill|sue ware|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|12=draft}} found on the terrace section, the kofun is believed to have been constructed in the late 7th to early 8th century. Restoration work was carried out in February 1987. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * Shisui Town and Inba District designation1 Center. _Excavation Report on the Shisui Town Comprehensive Park Site: Onoue Hirohata Ruins and Sumikomorida Kofun._ 1987. == See Also == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧|12=draft}} {{japanese-history-stub}} [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Sumiko Morita Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Sumiko Morita Kofun]][[Category:qq]] {{draft categories| [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Sumiko Morita Kofun]] [[Category:Kofun in Chiba Prefecture]] [[Category:Rectangular Kofun]] [[Category:History of Shisui, Chiba]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:return the see also]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:New2]] [[Category:qq]]{{Kofun navbar}}[[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Sumiko Morita Kofun]] [[ja:墨小盛田古墳]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Sumiko Morita Kofun}}

Rectangular Kofuns are a characteristic type of kofun found in the {{ill|Shimousa Province|ja|下総国|12=draft}} region during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|12=draft}}. In November 1986, during the construction of the {{ill|Shisui Comprehensive Park|ja|酒々井総合公園|12=draft}}, a one-month excavation was conducted. The mound features a 2-meter-wide surrounding moat and a protruding section. The main burial chamber, located at the center of the southern side of the mound, was identified as a {{ill|horizontal stone burial chamber|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=draft}}. Based on the {{ill|sue ware|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|12=draft}} found on the terrace section, the kofun is believed to have been constructed in the late 7th to early 8th century. Restoration work was carried out in February 1987. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * Shisui Town and Inba District designation1 Center. _Excavation Report on the Shisui Town Comprehensive Park Site: Onoue Hirohata Ruins and Sumikomorida Kofun._ 1987. == See Also == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧|12=draft}} {{japanese-history-stub}} [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Sumiko Morita Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Sumiko Morita Kofun]][[Category:qq]] {{draft categories| [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Sumiko Morita Kofun]] [[Category:Kofun in Chiba Prefecture]] [[Category:Rectangular Kofun]] [[Category:History of Shisui, Chiba]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:return the see also]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:qq]]{{Kofun navbar}}[[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Sumiko Morita Kofun]] [[ja:墨小盛田古墳]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Sumiko Morita Kofun}}

In November 1986, during the construction of the {{ill|Shisui Comprehensive Park|ja|酒々井総合公園|12=draft}}, a one-month excavation was conducted. revision.text:
{{日本の古墳 |名称=墨小盛田古墳 |画像 =[[File:Sumikomorida Kofun.JPG|250px]] |画像キャプション =墳丘 |所在地=[[千葉県]][[印旛郡]][[酒々井町]][[墨 (酒々井町)|墨]]字盛田36-1 |史跡=指定なし |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|43|18.3|N|140|16|46.0|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} |形状=方墳 |規模=30メートル×23メートル |築造年代=7世紀末-8世紀 |地図2 = {{Location map|Japan Chiba|label=墨小盛田
古墳|lat_deg=35|lat_min=43|lat_sec=18.3|lon_deg=140|lon_min=16|lon_sec=46.0|mark=方墳.svg|marksize=25|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=千葉県内の位置}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|43|18.3|N|140|16|46.0|E}}}} }} '''墨小盛田古墳'''（すみこもりだこふん/すみこもったこふん{{Cite web|和書|author=酒々井町役場|url=https://www.town.shisui.chiba.jp/navi/mobile/id/374.html|title=墨小盛田古墳|work=酒々井観光ナビ|accessdate=2020-08-20}}）は、[[千葉県]][[印旛郡]][[酒々井町]][[墨 (酒々井町)|墨]]にある[[方墳]]である。 ==概要== 南北辺約30メートル、東西辺約23メートル、高さ3メートルの長方墳である。長方墳は、[[古墳時代]]の末に[[下総]]地域にみられる特徴的な古墳である。 [[酒々井総合公園]]の建設に伴い1986年（昭和61年）11月から約1か月間の調査が行われた。幅2メールの周溝が巡り、張り出しが付く。主体部は墳丘南中央で[[横口式石槨]]が確認された。テラス部から出土した[[須恵器]]より[[7世紀]]末から[[8世紀]]の築造と考えられている。1987年（昭和62年）2月に復元工事が行われた。 == 脚注 == {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == * 酒々井町・印旛郡市文化財センター 「酒々井町総合公園遺跡発掘調査報告書 -尾上広畑遺跡・墨小盛田古墳- 」 1987年 == 関連項目 == *[[日本の古墳一覧]] {{japanese-history-stub}} {{デフォルトソート:すみこもりたこふん}} [[Category:千葉県の古墳]] [[Category:長方形墳]] [[Category:酒々井町の歴史]]

Kofun|coordinates=35|coordinates=43|coordinates=18.3|lon_deg=140|lon_min=16|lon_sec=46.0|mark=方墳.svg|marksize=25|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location within Chiba Prefecture}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|43|18.3|N|140|16|46.0|E}}}} |architectural_styles = {{ill|Q80793203|qid=Q80793203}} }} '''Sumikomorida Kofun''' (墨小盛田古墳, also pronounced as Sumiko Morita Kofun{{Cite web|和書|author=Shisui Town Hall|url=https://www.town.shisui.chiba.jp/navi/mobile/id/374.html|title=Sumikomorida Kofun|work=Shisui Tourism Navi|accessdate=2020-08-20}}) is a {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} located in [[:en:Shisui|Shisui, Chiba]], [[:en:Chiba Prefecture|Chiba Prefecture]], Japan. == Overview == The Kofun measures approximately 30 meters north-south, 23 meters east-west, and 3 meters in height. {{ill|Rectangular Kofun|lt=Rectangular Kofuns|ja|長方形墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rectangular Kofun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} are a characteristic type of kofun found in the [[:en:Shimōsa Province|Shimousa Province]] region during the late [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]]. In November 1986, during the construction of the {{ill|Shisui Comprehensive Park|ja|酒々井総合公園|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shisui Comprehensive Park|qq=|1=Shisui Comprehensive Park|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11643985}}, a one-month excavation was conducted. The mound features a 2-meter-wide surrounding moat and a protruding section. The main burial chamber, located at the center of the southern side of the mound, was identified as a {{ill|horizontal stone burial chamber|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/horizontal stone burial chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=}}. Based on the [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] found on the terrace section, the kofun is believed to have been constructed in the late 7th to early 8th century. Restoration work was carried out in February 1987. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * Shisui Town and Inba District designation1 Center. _Excavation Report on the Shisui Town Comprehensive Park Site: Onoue Hirohata Ruins and Sumikomorida Kofun._ 1987. == See Also == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of kofun in Japan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} {{japanese-history-stub}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|墨小盛田古墳|version=97964219|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 5]] }} [[ja:墨小盛田古墳]] [[Category:Kofun of Chiba prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal History of Shisui]] [[Category:Rectangular Kofun]] [[Category:Rectangular Kofun]] {{wikidata link|Q17220290}}

Kofun|coordinates=35|coordinates=43|coordinates=18.3|lon_deg=140|lon_min=16|lon_sec=46.0|mark=方墳.svg|marksize=25|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location within Chiba Prefecture}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|43|18.3|N|140|16|46.0|E}}}} |architectural_styles = {{ill|Q80793203|WD=Q80793203}} }} '''Sumikomorida Kofun''' (墨小盛田古墳, also pronounced as Sumiko Morita Kofun{{Cite web|和書|author=Shisui Town Hall|url=https://www.town.shisui.chiba.jp/navi/mobile/id/374.html|title=Sumikomorida Kofun|work=Shisui Tourism Navi|accessdate=2020-08-20}}) is a {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} located in [[:en:Shisui|Shisui, Chiba]], [[:en:Chiba Prefecture|Chiba Prefecture]], Japan. == Overview == The Kofun measures approximately 30 meters north-south, 23 meters east-west, and 3 meters in height. {{ill|Rectangular Kofun|lt=Rectangular Kofuns|ja|長方形墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rectangular Kofun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} are a characteristic type of kofun found in the [[:en:Shimōsa Province|Shimousa Province]] region during the late [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]]. In November 1986, during the construction of the {{ill|Shisui Comprehensive Park|ja|酒々井総合公園|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shisui Comprehensive Park|qq=|1=Shisui Comprehensive Park|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11643985}}, a one-month excavation was conducted. The mound features a 2-meter-wide surrounding moat and a protruding section. The main burial chamber, located at the center of the southern side of the mound, was identified as a {{ill|horizontal stone burial chamber|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/horizontal stone burial chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=}}. Based on the [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] found on the terrace section, the kofun is believed to have been constructed in the late 7th to early 8th century. Restoration work was carried out in February 1987. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * Shisui Town and Inba District designation1 Center. _Excavation Report on the Shisui Town Comprehensive Park Site: Onoue Hirohata Ruins and Sumikomorida Kofun._ 1987. == See Also == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of kofun in Japan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} {{japanese-history-stub}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|墨小盛田古墳|version=97964219|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 5]] }} [[ja:墨小盛田古墳]] [[Category:Kofun of Chiba prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal History of Shisui]] [[Category:Rectangular Kofun]] [[Category:Rectangular Kofun]] {{wikidata link|Q17220290}}

Kofun|coordinates=35|coordinates=43|coordinates=18.3|lon_deg=140|lon_min=16|lon_sec=46.0|mark=方墳.svg|marksize=25|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location within Chiba Prefecture}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|43|18.3|N|140|16|46.0|E}}}} |architectural_styles = {{ill|Q80793203|WD=Q80793203}} }} '''Sumikomorida Kofun''' (墨小盛田古墳, also pronounced as Sumiko Morita Kofun{{Cite web|和書|author=Shisui Town Hall|url=https://www.town.shisui.chiba.jp/navi/mobile/id/374.html|title=Sumikomorida Kofun|work=Shisui Tourism Navi|accessdate=2020-08-20}}) is a {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} located in [[:en:Shisui|Shisui, Chiba]], [[:en:Chiba Prefecture|Chiba Prefecture]], Japan. == Overview == The Kofun measures approximately 30 meters north-south, 23 meters east-west, and 3 meters in height. {{ill|Rectangular Kofun|lt=Rectangular Kofuns|ja|長方形墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rectangular Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} are a characteristic type of kofun found in the [[:en:Shimōsa Province|Shimousa Province]] region during the late [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]]. In November 1986, during the construction of the {{ill|Shisui Comprehensive Park|ja|酒々井総合公園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shisui Comprehensive Park|1=Shisui Comprehensive Park|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11643985}}, a one-month excavation was conducted. The mound features a 2-meter-wide surrounding moat and a protruding section. The main burial chamber, located at the center of the southern side of the mound, was identified as a {{ill|horizontal stone burial chamber|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/horizontal stone burial chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=}}. Based on the [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] found on the terrace section, the kofun is believed to have been constructed in the late 7th to early 8th century. Restoration work was carried out in February 1987. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * Shisui Town and Inba District designation1 Center. _Excavation Report on the Shisui Town Comprehensive Park Site: Onoue Hirohata Ruins and Sumikomorida Kofun._ 1987. == See Also == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of kofun in Japan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} {{japanese-history-stub}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|墨小盛田古墳|version=97964219|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 5]] }} [[ja:墨小盛田古墳]] [[Category:Kofun of Chiba prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal History of Shisui]] [[Category:Rectangular Kofun]] [[Category:Rectangular Kofun]] {{wikidata link|Q17220290}}

Kofun|coordinates=35|coordinates=43|coordinates=18.3|lon_deg=140|lon_min=16|lon_sec=46.0|mark=方墳.svg|marksize=25|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location within Chiba Prefecture}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|43|18.3|N|140|16|46.0|E}}}} |architectural_styles = {{ill|Q80793203|WD=Q80793203}} }} '''Sumikomorida Kofun''' (墨小盛田古墳, also pronounced as Sumiko Morita Kofun{{Cite web|和書|author=Shisui Town Hall|url=https://www.town.shisui.chiba.jp/navi/mobile/id/374.html|title=Sumikomorida Kofun|work=Shisui Tourism Navi|accessdate=2020-08-20}}) is a {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} located in [[:en:Shisui|Shisui, Chiba]], [[:en:Chiba Prefecture|Chiba Prefecture]], Japan. == Overview == The Kofun measures approximately 30 meters north-south, 23 meters east-west, and 3 meters in height. {{ill|Rectangular Kofun|lt=Rectangular Kofuns|ja|長方形墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rectangular Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} are a characteristic type of kofun found in the [[:en:Shimōsa Province|Shimousa Province]] region during the late [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]]. In November 1986, during the construction of the {{ill|Shisui Comprehensive Park|ja|酒々井総合公園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shisui Comprehensive Park|1=Shisui Comprehensive Park|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11643985}}, a one-month excavation was conducted. The mound features a 2-meter-wide surrounding moat and a protruding section. The main burial chamber, located at the center of the southern side of the mound, was identified as a {{ill|horizontal stone burial chamber|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/horizontal stone burial chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=}}. Based on the [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] found on the terrace section, the kofun is believed to have been constructed in the late 7th to early 8th century. Restoration work was carried out in February 1987. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * Shisui Town and Inba District designation1 Center. _Excavation Report on the Shisui Town Comprehensive Park Site: Onoue Hirohata Ruins and Sumikomorida Kofun._ 1987. == See Also == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of kofun in Japan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} {{japanese-history-stub}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|墨小盛田古墳|version=97964219|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 5]] }} [[ja:墨小盛田古墳]] [[Category:Kofun of Chiba prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal History of Shisui]] [[Category:Rectangular Kofun]] [[Category:Rectangular Kofun]] {{wikidata link|Q17220290}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

Kofun|coordinates=35|coordinates=43|coordinates=18.3|lon_deg=140|lon_min=16|lon_sec=46.0|mark=方墳.svg|marksize=25|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location within Chiba Prefecture}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|43|18.3|N|140|16|46.0|E}}}} |architectural_styles = {{ill|Q80793203|WD=Q80793203}} }} '''Sumikomorida Kofun''' (墨小盛田古墳, also pronounced as Sumiko Morita Kofun{{Cite web|和書|author=Shisui Town Hall|url=https://www.town.shisui.chiba.jp/navi/mobile/id/374.html|title=Sumikomorida Kofun|work=Shisui Tourism Navi|accessdate=2020-08-20}}) is a {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} located in [[:en:Shisui|Shisui, Chiba]], [[:en:Chiba Prefecture|Chiba Prefecture]], Japan. == Overview == The Kofun measures approximately 30 meters north-south, 23 meters east-west, and 3 meters in height. {{ill|Rectangular Kofun|lt=Rectangular Kofuns|ja|長方形墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rectangular Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} are a characteristic type of kofun found in the [[:en:Shimōsa Province|Shimousa Province]] region during the late [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]]. In November 1986, during the construction of the {{ill|Shisui Comprehensive Park|ja|酒々井総合公園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shisui Comprehensive Park|1=Shisui Comprehensive Park|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11643985}}, a one-month excavation was conducted. The mound features a 2-meter-wide surrounding moat and a protruding section. The main burial chamber, located at the center of the southern side of the mound, was identified as a {{ill|horizontal stone burial chamber|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/horizontal stone burial chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=}}. Based on the [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] found on the terrace section, the kofun is believed to have been constructed in the late 7th to early 8th century. Restoration work was carried out in February 1987. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * Shisui Town and Inba District designation1 Center. _Excavation Report on the Shisui Town Comprehensive Park Site: Onoue Hirohata Ruins and Sumikomorida Kofun._ 1987. == See Also == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of kofun in Japan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} {{japanese-history-stub}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|墨小盛田古墳|version=97964219|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 5]] }} [[ja:墨小盛田古墳]] [[Category:Kofun of Chiba prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal History of Shisui]] [[Category:Rectangular Kofun]] [[Category:Rectangular Kofun]] {{wikidata link|Q17220290}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]]

Kofun|coordinates=35|coordinates=43|coordinates=18.3|lon_deg=140|lon_min=16|lon_sec=46.0|mark=方墳.svg|marksize=25|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location within Chiba Prefecture}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|43|18.3|N|140|16|46.0|E}}}} |architectural_styles = {{ill|Q80793203|WD=Q80793203}} }} '''Sumikomorida Kofun''' (墨小盛田古墳, also pronounced as Sumiko Morita Kofun{{Cite web|和書|author=Shisui Town Hall|url=https://www.town.shisui.chiba.jp/navi/mobile/id/374.html|title=Sumikomorida Kofun|work=Shisui Tourism Navi|accessdate=2020-08-20}}) is a {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} located in [[:en:Shisui|Shisui, Chiba]], [[:en:Chiba Prefecture|Chiba Prefecture]], Japan. == Overview == The Kofun measures approximately 30 meters north-south, 23 meters east-west, and 3 meters in height. {{ill|Rectangular Kofun|lt=Rectangular Kofuns|ja|長方形墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rectangular Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} are a characteristic type of kofun found in the [[:en:Shimōsa Province|Shimousa Province]] region during the late [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]]. In November 1986, during the construction of the {{ill|Shisui Comprehensive Park|ja|酒々井総合公園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shisui Comprehensive Park|1=Shisui Comprehensive Park|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11643985}}, a one-month excavation was conducted. The mound features a 2-meter-wide surrounding moat and a protruding section. The main burial chamber, located at the center of the southern side of the mound, was identified as a {{ill|horizontal stone burial chamber|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/horizontal stone burial chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=}}. Based on the [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] found on the terrace section, the kofun is believed to have been constructed in the late 7th to early 8th century. Restoration work was carried out in February 1987. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * Shisui Town and Inba District designation1 Center. _Excavation Report on the Shisui Town Comprehensive Park Site: Onoue Hirohata Ruins and Sumikomorida Kofun._ 1987. == See Also == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of kofun in Japan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} {{japanese-history-stub}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|墨小盛田古墳|version=97964219|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:墨小盛田古墳]] {{wikidata link|Q17220290}} [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Kofun of Chiba prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal History of Shisui]] [[Category:Rectangular Kofun]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]

revision.text:
[[ja:墨小盛田古墳]] [[ja:墨小盛田古墳]] [[ja:墨小盛田古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Rectangular Kofun|ja|長方形墳|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rectangular Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |name = Sumikomorida Kofun |image = File:Sumikomorida Kofun.JPG |imageキャプション = Kofun |location = {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|en|Chiba Prefecture|WD=Q80011|lt=Chiba Prefecture}}, {{ill|Inba District|en|Inba District|WD=Q677245|lt=Inba District, Chiba}}, {{ill|Shisui|en|Shisui|WD=Q782157|lt=Shisui, Chiba}}, {{ill|Sumi (Shisui)|ja|墨 (酒々井町)|lt=Sumi|1=Sumi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17220257}} Aza Morita 36-1 |史跡 = None |coordinates = {{Coord|35.72175|N|140.279444|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |type = {{ill|Rectangular Kofun|ja|長方形墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rectangular Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |area = 30m (north-south) × 23m (east-west) |built = Late 7th century to 8th century |地図2 = {{location map|Japan Chiba|map_dot_label=Sumikomorida
Kofun|coordinates=35|coordinates=43|coordinates=18.3|lon_deg=140|lon_min=16|lon_sec=46.0|mark=方墳.svg|marksize=25|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location within Chiba Prefecture}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|43|18.3|N|140|16|46.0|E}}}} |architectural_styles = {{ill|Q80793203|WD=Q80793203}} }} '''Sumikomorida Kofun''' (墨小盛田古墳, also pronounced as Sumiko Morita Kofun{{Cite web|和書|author=Shisui Town Hall|url=https://www.town.shisui.chiba.jp/navi/mobile/id/374.html|title=Sumikomorida Kofun|work=Shisui Tourism Navi|accessdate=2020-08-20}}) is a {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} located in {{ill|Shisui|en|Shisui|WD=Q782157|lt=Shisui, Chiba}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|en|Chiba Prefecture|WD=Q80011|lt=Chiba Prefecture}}, Japan. == Overview == The Kofun measures approximately 30 meters north-south, 23 meters east-west, and 3 meters in height. {{ill|Rectangular Kofun|lt=Rectangular Kofuns|ja|長方形墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rectangular Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} are a characteristic type of kofun found in the {{ill|Shimōsa Province|en|Shimōsa Province|WD=Q1053695|lt=Shimousa Province}} region during the late {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|WD=Q459939|lt=Kofun period}}. In November 1986, during the construction of the {{ill|Shisui Comprehensive Park|ja|酒々井総合公園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shisui Comprehensive Park|1=Shisui Comprehensive Park|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11643985}}, a one-month excavation was conducted. The mound features a 2-meter-wide surrounding moat and a protruding section. The main burial chamber, located at the center of the southern side of the mound, was identified as a {{ill|horizontal stone burial chamber|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/horizontal stone burial chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=}}. Based on the {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|WD=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery}} found on the terrace section, the kofun is believed to have been constructed in the late 7th to early 8th century. Restoration work was carried out in February 1987. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * Shisui Town and Inba District designation1 Center. _Excavation Report on the Shisui Town Comprehensive Park Site: Onoue Hirohata Ruins and Sumikomorida Kofun._ 1987. == See Also == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|Infobox ancient site一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of kofun in Japan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} {{japanese-history-stub}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|墨小盛田古墳|version=97964219|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:墨小盛田古墳]] {{wikidata link|Q17220290}} [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Kofun of Chiba prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal History of Shisui]] [[Category:Rectangular Kofun]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]
